emails.subject: RE: Need some inspiration getting started with Pica? Well, I could use some detailed instructions on how to integrate with QuickBooks running on-premise...?
emails.body:
Hi Erol,

Thanks for reaching out. Good question.

Pica's native QuickBooks integration supports QuickBooks Online. QuickBooks Desktop/Enterprise on-premise is a different beast -- it uses XML/SOAP protocols (qbXML via the Desktop SDK or Web Connector), not REST APIs, so we don't support it directly.

That said, there is a solid path for your use case. We support Conductor, which provides a modern API layer on top of QuickBooks Desktop. Through Conductor + Pica, your SaaS app could push invoices and estimates to QB Enterprise on-prem without dealing with the XML/SOAP plumbing yourself.

I'd suggest hopping on a quick call with Jacob on our team to walk through how this would work for your setup. You can grab time here: https://calendly.com/jacob-picaos/30min

Let me know if you have any questions in the meantime.

Best,
Moe
emails.snippet: Hi Erol, Thanks for reaching out. Good question. Pica's native QuickBooks integration supports QuickBooks Online. QuickBooks Desktop/Enterprise on-premise is a different beast -- it uses XML/SOAP

emails.subject: 🌍 The Community Edition: The 73M View Blueprint + The Rise of the Scientist Simulator
emails.body:
View this post on the web at https://aicollective.substack.com/p/the-community-edition-the-73m-view

Itâs Friday, February 13th: This week, weâre dissecting the new "applied AI" playbookâfrom Matt Shumerâs viral breakdown of the reasoning frontier to the technical shift toward inference-time scaling with the Scientist Simulator, plus on-the-ground insights from our builders in DC and Paris.
Head over to our Events Portal [ https://substack.com/redirect/bb86271d-d2d8-4987-989d-f921f6ea17f3?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] to get the latest on upcoming AI Collective events near you. Search by city, date, or event format, and join thousands of builders at events across 100+ chapters on every continent (except Antarctica, for now). 
ð Based in SF? Check out SF IRL [ https://substack.com/redirect/e4373c83-d5fe-48b0-8fe3-5730b7e6f7d7?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ], MLOps SF [ https://substack.com/redirect/43162171-ab66-456e-b7f1-37e0ce1d5a29?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ], GenerativeAISF [ https://substack.com/redirect/e047877c-c090-4bfd-b37f-386470d822b9?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ], or Cerebral Valleyâs spreadsheet [ https://substack.com/redirect/80a3a007-a875-409e-a643-3f766562b90f?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] for more!
1ï¸â£ The 73M View Blueprint: Matt Shumer on the Death of âTechnical Workâ
Matt Shumerâs latest piece, âSomething Big Is Happening [ https://substack.com/redirect/ef231bb5-2372-4cd6-a6ac-82618de63b3e?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ],â has exploded across X, racking up 73 million impressions in just a few days. Why the viral heat? Because Matt is calling out the âcocktail-party versionâ of AI news and replacing it with a raw look at the frontier.
He argues that weâve crossed the threshold into Reinforcement Learning on Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), where models like GPT-5.3 Codex arenât just predicting textâtheyâre executing judgment. For the builder community, the message is clear: the moat isnât the code anymore; itâs your ability to architect the vision. If youâre still using 2024-era âvanillaâ models, youâre already a year behind.
2ï¸â£ The âScientist Simulatorâ and the End of the Data Wall
This one marks a fundamental shift in how we think about AI scaling. For years, the industry mantra was âmore data, more compute, more intelligence.â But weâre finally hitting the âData Wallââweâve run out of high-quality human text to feed the machines.
The Scientist Simulator [ https://substack.com/redirect/d2453167-f104-47b0-b805-f4f9128a0027?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] suggests a radical way out: stop training on what humans said and start training on what the AI discovers.
The core idea is Test-Time Training (TTT). Instead of the model giving you a fast, âreflexâ answer, it enters a simulation loop. It acts as an agent that proposes a hypothesis, writes code to test it in a sandbox, observes the result, and iteratesâall before it ever types a word back to you.
This matters because it transforms the LLM from a âknowledge databaseâ into a scientific collaborator. Itâs the move from âSystem 1â (fast, intuitive, often wrong) to âSystem 2â (slow, analytical, self-correcting) thinking. Much like the community stepping up for Tailwind, this shift shows that the future of AI isnât just in the hands of the labsâitâs in the architectures and âverification loopsâ that we, the builders, design.
Join 150K+ AI Collective members getting curated news, tools, and events every week. Subscribe to stay ahead.
Each week, we highlight AI Collective chapters doing groundbreaking work with their members around the world. Tag us on socials to be featured!
ðï¸ DC | The AI Collective Demo Night: Beyond the Pitch
The energy at our DC Demo Night [ https://substack.com/redirect/4e607c2d-e8e3-4195-91a9-6753a0307ed1?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] focused on the raw reality of shipping. As David C. Coffey noted, the room prioritized âhow it worksâ over âwhat if.â
The Applied AI Takeaway:
Closing Loops: Builders demonstrated how they are tackling the âjagged performanceâ of LLMs through verifiable utility, moving past theory and into production-grade tools.
Flywheel Effect: The night wasnât just about demos; it was a conversion machine for founders connecting with early hires and technical collaborators.
If you are building in the DMV area, donât watch from the sidelines. Join our DC Chapter [ https://substack.com/redirect/e8520b35-0573-463e-b3fa-c0890d5b9c0f?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] to get inside these feedback loops.
ð«ð· Paris | 2026 AI Predictions: Scaling the "Summoned Ghosts"
Founder Mixer with Paatch [ https://substack.com/redirect/f1415b2e-7147-4a55-8fea-26f99a1bb847?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] in Paris cut through the hype to focus on the immediate 2026 Shift. As Louis-Nicolas Roussel captured, the conversation prioritized the engineering reality of what happens after the initial model excitement fades.
The Strategy:
Applied AI at Scale: Founders focused on moving past âcool demosâ to address the 2026 bottleneck: how to turn raw model intelligence into a flywheel for production-grade systems.
Closing Loops: The consensus in the room was that the next era of growth belongs to builders who can architect reliable agentic workflows that solve complex, multi-step reasoning tasks.
Conversion machine: This wasnât just a mixer; it was a high-signal environment for founders to connect with technical partners and operators ready to execute on these predictions.
Donât just read the forecast. Shape it. Join the Paris Chapter [ https://substack.com/redirect/9f1f6e60-691c-42c2-8c04-5064c056c8f3?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] to get inside these high-velocity feedback loops and start building for the 2026 frontier.
Our Premier Partner: Roam [ https://substack.com/redirect/44b7b423-3c58-497f-83f3-5ddc3f61d14b?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ]
Roam is the virtual workspace our team relies on to stay connected across time zones. It makes collaboration feel natural with shared spaces, private rooms, and built-in AI tools.
Roamâs focus on human-centered collaboration is why theyâre our Premier Partner, supporting our mission to connect the builders and leaders shaping the future of AI.
Experience Roam yourself with a free 14-day trial!
â¡ï¸ Before You Go
Partner With Us
Launching a new product or hosting an event? Put your work in front of our global audience of builders, founders, and operators â we feature select products and announcements that offer real value to our readers.
ð To be featured or sponsor a placement, reach out to our team.
The AI Collective is a community of volunteers, made for volunteers. All proceeds directly fund future initiatives that benefit this community.

ð§âð¼ LinkedIn: The AI Collective [ https://substack.com/redirect/1f4e4df4-3fd2-46fa-9182-d04aa9b8fd4f?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ]

ð· Join the Team [ https://substack.com/redirect/30e4264e-9028-4ab6-acc6-e25f85b51a3a?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ]
About the Authors

Joy is a news editor, writer, and entrepreneur at the forefront of the emerging tech landscape. A former educator turned media strategist, she demystifies complex systems to make AI and blockchain accessible for all. emails.subject: Re: What are you building with Pica?
emails.body:
Hi there,

â

Thanks for reaching out!

â

Iâd recommend you check out one of our use case videos and try them out while following the guide.

â

You can watch videos/tutorials on our website by clicking on this link: https://docs.picaos.com/use-cases

â

Additionally, we also did a livestream with bolt that shows a live demo building a production-ready app from scratch. Feel free to follow along while watching the livestream here:

â

Part 1: https://www.youtube.com/live/ipi3DeQIRDY?si=jbtMdffX8VqdE7MO

emails.subject: Sheriff shakeup for M&A
emails.body:
VC steers AI into specific sectors; Anthropic edges by OpenAI with $30B Series G; half of unicorn value held by top 10
February 13, 2026   |   Read online   |   Manage your subscription
The Daily Pitch
VC
Presented by SVB
 
AI's next phase: Venture capital is increasingly focused on putting AI to work. VCs are now investing more in AI applications for specific industries. Key numbers from our latest AI VC Trends report:
  • In Q4, vertical applications attracted $22 billion across 1,151 deals, surpassing horizontal platforms, which recorded over $16 billion across 408 pacts.
  • The shift was notable given horizontal platforms' $31.3 billion in Q3, underscoring a sharp sequential pullback at the model and automation layer.
Download the report
 
What DOJ shakeup could mean for tech M&A
(Andrew Harnik/Getty Images)
By Rosie Bradbury, Senior Venture Capital Reporter

Gail Slater, who has led the Justice Department's antitrust division for the last 11 months, is stepping down.

She said she made the decision on her own, but multiple news outlets report that she was pushed out by the Trump administration.

Her ousting could mark a turning point: She has been a vocal advocate against tech monopolies and reportedly butted heads with Attorney General Pam Bondi and other high-ranking members of the administration, particularly over Hewlett Packard Enterprise's recent acquisition of Juniper Networks.

The Trump administration has indicated a more friendly attitude toward M&A than its predecessor, having greenlit several mega-deals, including Netflixâs recent $82.7 billion deal with Warner Bros.

Slater will leave behind several active lawsuits, including a pair against Alphabet's Google and Apple over allegedly anti-competitive practices.

For VCs hungry for M&A dealmaking, Slater's exit will be welcome news. Under Slater, and her Biden-era predecessor, more tech giants carried out âacqui-hireâ deals in the race for AI talent to avoid antitrust scrutiny. Such deals have come with mixed reactions from VCs.

Still, M&A activity has been on a tear, with 2025 surpassing 2021 as the biggest year on record both globally and in North America, per PitchBook's 2025 Global M&A report.

Omeed Assefi, Slater's deputy, was named acting antitrust chief, CBS News reported.
Share this story
 
Related research: 2025 Annual Global M&A report
 
A message from SVB  
SVB releases 2026 State of the Markets
 
SVBâs new report finds venture gaining steam â $340B invested and exits are up â while fundraising tightens and round timelines stretch.

Get the full report
 
Catch Up Quick  
â¢ Anthropic's $30 billion Series G puts it just ahead of OpenAI in the AI fundraising race, intensifying competitive pressure between the two biggest LLM developers as they race to go public sometime this year. Find out more

â¢ Startup valuations have never been this top-heavy, with the 10 largest startups now controlling a record 51.8% of total unicorn value. Read more

â¢ US investors' interest in Europe continues to grow as they seek to diversify from their crowded home market and look for deals trading at a discount. See which countries benefited
 
Nuveen enters PE big leagues with Schroders acquisition
By Rod James, Senior Private Equity Editor

The Â£9.9 billion ($13.3 billion) acquisition of Schroders by Nuveen is not only one of the largest ever acquisitions of an asset management firm, but it also has significant implications for the competitive dynamics of the private markets.

The combined entity will have $414 billion of private AUM, placing it among the 10 largest pools of illiquid capital in the world. It might place even higher if public assets managed by the other firms on the list are subtracted from their AUM totals, a tricky task.
 
Schroders has around $111 billion in private assets across equity, real assets and credit. Among the PE offerings it brings to the table is a $25.5 billion investment program that makes primary commitments and co-investments in lower-middle-market and mid-market buyout, growth and VC funds.

It also has a series of funds focused on smaller GP-led secondary deals. The unit had invested $3.5 billion as of June 30, Schroders said.

Among other properties, Nuveen owns Churchill Asset Management, a $63 billion investment firm focused on mid-market senior and junior lending in North America. And in 2022, it acquired one of Europe's largest independent private debt managers, Arcmont Asset Management, bringing in an additional $21 billion of committed capital.

Even with all these properties, private markets will account for only roughly 17% of the combined entity, whose total AUM is around $2.5 trillion.

The combination of Nuveen and Schroders represents the second-largest M&A deal involving a financial asset manager since 2015, according to PitchBook data. The largest was also centered on a Europe-based firm, Aberdeen Asset Management, which combined with Standard Life in 2017 to try and match its US rivals for scale. Of the top five on the list, three were done, in part, to boost the acquirer's private credit AUM.
Read the full story
 
Related article: Nuveen's $13.3B deal for 222-year-old Schroders bolsters its public-to-private offering
 
Side Letters  
Smart reads that caught our eye.

â¢ Forget the K-shaped economy, and start visualizing the letter E instead. New analysis shows that middle-class consumers are on a unique path, creating a distinction between the wealthy and low-income categories. [Fortune]

â¢ Even as Saudi Arabiaâs economy booms, many young Saudis face stagnant wages, rising costs and a fraying social contract under Vision 2030. [The Economist]

â¢ A Paris trial lays bare the alleged mechanics of a multimillion-euro insider trading scheme tied to Air Liquideâs takeover of Airgas. [Bloomberg]

People  
Moelis appointed Kyle Reidy as managing director to lead its private credit secondaries unit.

Olympian Elieen Gu is joining Benchmark as a senior associate when the Winter Games conclude in Milan.
 
VC Deals  
Modal Labs, an AI inference software developer, is in talks to raise a new round led by General Catalyst at a $2.5 billion valuation, TechCrunch reported.

Psychiatric care services provider Talkiatry raised a $210 million Series D led by Perceptive Advisors.

Stockholm-based pet insurer Lassie raised $75 million from investors including Felix Capital, Balderton Capital and Stena Sessan.

Ireland-based medtech startup Neurent Medical secured a â¬62.5 million Series C led by MVM Partners.

Anterior, the developer of a workflow platform for health plans, received a $40 million round from investors including NEA and Sequoia.

AI sales platform developer Monaco raised a total of $35 million: a $10 million seed round and a $25 million Series A both led by Founders Fund.

Nuclear energy startup Alva Energy secured a $33 million Series A led by Playground Global.

Ever, a startup building an online marketplace for EVs, raised a $31 million Series A led by Eclipse.

Blockbrain, a German AI startup enabling companies to securely digitize expertise, processes and decision-making logic, received a â¬17.5 million Series A led by Alstin Capital and 13books Capital.

Electric Twin, a London-based developer of synthetic audiences to model customer behavior, raised $10 million in a round led by Atomico.
 
PE Deals  
Blackstone and EQT agreed to buy Urbaser, a Madrid-based waste management company, from Platinum Equity for $6.6 billion.

Cinven invested in Linden Capital Partners-backed Regenity Biosciences, a New Jersey-based developer of biomaterial products for healthcare applications.

Coral Tree Partners invested in Dallas-based Plein Air, which provides marketing services to the restaurant and retail industries.

Merieux Equity Partners acquired a minority stake in Health in Code, a Spanish clinical genomics service provider.

Bridgepoint-backed SK AeroSafety Group purchased Frontline SRL, an Italian aircraft components maintenance, repairs and operations provider.
 
Exits  
PayPay, backed by SoftBank, filed publicly for its US IPO. The Japanese mobile payments group is seeking a valuation of over $10 billion, Bloomberg reported.

Deutsche Boerse agreed to acquire General Atlantic's â¬1.1 billion 20% stake in Frankfurt-based capital markets data provider ISS Stoxx.
 
Fundraising  
Oaktree Capital Management raised $2.4 billion at the first close of its fourth Special Situations Fund, which is targeting as much as $5 billion in total commitments, Bloomberg reported.

Seligman Ventures launched a $500 million fund focused on early-stage investments in AI startups, Fortune reported.
 
Chart of the Day

emails.subject: Vector |  Meta bets on facial recognition
emails.body:
----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ba05790b-b8f9-4f69-a529-98b84cf7c990/Subscribers_only_with_lock__transparent_.png?t=1765488456)
Caption: 


---------- 

**Welcome back.**

â_that startup guy_ is now _Vector_. 

Weâll spare you the brand manifesto âÂ same writer, same focus, better name.â â 

Meta apparently believes the best time to revive a controversial technology is when nobody's watching â internal memos show the company plans to bring facial recognition to its bestselling Ray-Ban smart glasses, banking on political chaos during the midterms to blunt the inevitable backlash.

Meanwhile, AI's disruption wave is finally hitting private equity where it hurts: the $500 billion-plus in software-linked debt that underpinned a decade of buyout riches is starting to look like the next shoe to drop.

Letâs get into it.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ad0901aa-343a-4655-be58-882aef602789/blue_bar__1_.png.png?t=1770931388)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/de672fc8-783d-4d10-8403-707cde1ddefd/meta.jpg?t=1770985358)
Follow image link: (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/13/technology/meta-facial-recognition-smart-glasses.html)
Caption: 




----------## _**Meta plans facial recognition for smart glasses**_

----------
Five years after shutting down facial recognition on Facebook amid privacy backlash, Meta is preparing to resurrect the technology â this time embedded in the smart glasses perched on your nose. The feature, internally dubbed "Name Tag," would let wearers identify people and pull up information about them through Meta's AI assistant, with a possible launch as soon as this year. 

An [internal memo](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/13/technology/meta-facial-recognition-smart-glasses.html\) obtained by the Times reveals the company views America's current political environment as strategic cover, noting that civil society groups "would have their resources focused on other concerns." The move comes as Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses have become a surprise hit, with partner EssilorLuxottica reporting more than seven million pairs sold last year.

**The scope question**
Meta is still deciding exactly who the glasses could identify â options range from people already connected to a user on Meta's platforms to anyone with a public Instagram account. The company says it won't function as a universal facial recognition tool, though two Harvard students already demonstrated in 2024 that third-party tools could turn the glasses into exactly that.

**"Super sensing" is the real play**
Beyond Name Tag, Meta is developing always-on glasses that would continuously run cameras and sensors to log a wearer's day â think AI meeting transcribers, but for your entire life. Facial recognition would be central to this vision, enabling the glasses to, say, remind you of a task when they spot a colleague. Zuckerberg has reportedly questioned whether the glasses even need their small LED recording indicator light for this mode.

**Privacy guardrails are loosening, not tightening**
In January 2025, Meta relaxed its internal privacy review process â the same process required by a $5 billion FTC settlement. A director of risk review told employees the changes would "push the bounds" of Meta's agreement with the agency, adding bluntly: "Mark wants to push on it a little bit."

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

**Why It Matters**:
Meta's facial recognition ambitions sit at the intersection of three powerful forces: a booming wearables market, an AI arms race that rewards richer context, and a regulatory environment that is, to put it diplomatically, distracted. 

The company has already paid $7 billion in privacy-related settlements, yet the commercial logic of differentiating its glasses from upcoming competitors like OpenAI's wearable appears to outweigh the legal caution. 

The internal memo's candid acknowledgment that political chaos provides launch cover is unusually revealing â companies rarely document the cynicism so clearly. If "super sensing" glasses become mainstream, the social contract around public anonymity shifts fundamentally: every face becomes queryable data, whether the person behind it consented or not.


---------- 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/d4c60788-73d9-49c0-a5c1-21242814fe49/Headlines.png?t=1770912710)
Caption: 

Youtube: Mustafa Suleyman sets out Microsoft AI's goal of 'humanist superintelligence' | FT Interview  (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YTrBz6Z5c0E)

[**â**](https://www.ft.com/content/f1ec830c-2f08-4b1a-b70f-7330f260753c)** Mustafa Suleyman plots AI âself-sufficiencyâ **
Mustafa Suleyman is selling independence from OpenAI like itâs a clean break, but Microsoftâs real advantage is contractual: it still has access to OpenAI IP through 2032, which gives Redmond a credible path to frontier work even if itâs starting late on the in-house model track



**[â](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-12/openai-accuses-deepseek-of-distilling-us-models-to-gain-an-edge?srnd=homepage-americas)**** OpenAI Claims DeepSeek Distilled US Models to Gain an Edge** 
OpenAI is accusing DeepSeek of âdistillingâ US models to leapfrog competitionâan argument that lands with a certain irony given OpenAIâs own internet-scale training diet, built in no small part on the unpaid labor of writers, artists, and everyone whoâs ever posted a decent paragraph online



[**â**](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-12/airbnb-sees-healthy-demand-fueling-faster-growth-in-2026?srnd=phx-technology)** Airbnb Sees âHealthyâ Demand Fueling Faster Growth in 2026** 
Airbnb says travel demand remains healthy and expects faster growth in 2026, essentially betting that incremental product improvements and disciplined marketing can outpace a market where differentiation increasingly looks like âsame inventory, different UIâ


---------- 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ad0901aa-343a-4655-be58-882aef602789/blue_bar__1_.png.png?t=1770931388)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/49e308ed-7992-48b6-becd-f6165094628b/pe.jpg?t=1770984663)
Follow image link: (https://vectornews.beehiiv.com/upgrade)
Caption: 




----------## _**Private Equityâs software bet is unraveling**_

----------
The AI revolution is rattling private equity's most beloved asset class. During the 2010s, buyout funds poured one in every three dollars into software companies, seduced by the predictable charm of recurring subscription revenue. 

Now AI-coding tools like Anthropic's Claude Code are threatening to upend incumbent software firms, sending listed software valuations down roughly 20% this year â and the tremors are reaching the murkier corners of private markets where â¦


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4fdf63c9-ad3a-4960-b2e3-2cb30f5c14aa/strategy_blue_anim_one_loop.gif?t=1753437621)
Caption: 


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ba05790b-b8f9-4f69-a529-98b84cf7c990/Subscribers_only_with_lock__transparent_.png?t=1765488456)
Caption: 

### Get the **Daily Pass** to read this 

Want full access to our best stuff? 

Start your free trial (https://vectornews.beehiiv.com/upgrade)

_**What youâre missing:**_

* _How AI is unraveling private equity's most reliable bet_

* _The hidden debt risk lurking behind software buyouts_

* _Why the next financial tremor may start with PE-backed software companies_


----------** ** 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/c9d29992-b11d-4fd8-ae25-33b0bf515172/VC_Corner.png?t=1770911081)
Caption:

Caption:

Caption:

_**What youâre missing:**_

* _What employee share sales reveal about timing_


----------  

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4b3afbf5-72ba-4c8f-bedb-eb4662cbfa98/Deals.png?t=1770911089)
Caption: 

----------
**[â](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-12/ai-startup-nabs-100-million-to-help-firms-predict-human-behavior?srnd=phx-technology)** **Simile Raises $100M** â Instead of guessing what customers (or analysts) will do next, Simile is building an AI âbehavior labâ that simulates human decision-makingâincluding the questions likely to land on earnings calls

**[â](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/12/didero-lands-30m-to-put-manufacturing-procurement-on-agentic-autopilot/)** **Didero Raises $30M** â Manufacturing procurement is a chaos smoothie of emails, WeChat threads, POs, and packing lists; Didero wants agents to drink it and spit out clean, automated workflows

**[â](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/12/eclipse-backs-all-ev-marketplace-ever-in-31m-funding-round/)** **Ever Raises $31M** â Ever is turning used-EV shopping into a marketplace product, aiming for an âorder a laptopâ experience in an industry that still feels like buying a timeshare

**[â](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260211301175/en/Loyal-Raises-%24100M-Series-C-Led-by-age1-to-Advance-the-First-Canine-Longevity-Drug)** **Loyal Raises $100M** â Loyal is taking the longevity obsession to its most emotionally defensible marketâdogsâby pushing toward what it hopes becomes the first FDA-approved drug designed to extend healthy canine lifespan

**[â](https://www.calcalistech.com/ctechnews/article/s1pb1gcdbl)** **Winn.AI Raises $18M** â On live sales calls, Winn.AI listens in real time, catches objections as they form, and serves reps the right answers from internal knowledge before the moment evaporates

**[â](https://www.finsmes.com/2026/02/uptiq-raises-25m-in-series-b-funding.html)** **Uptiq Raises $25M** â Uptiq is pitching banks and credit unions AI agents that can actually survive compliance, targeting regulated workflows like lending, risk, and wealthânot just another âcool demoâ that dies in legal review

**[â](https://www.businessinsider.com/vybe-pitch-deck-expand-vibe-coding-corporate-world-2026-2)** **Vybe Raises $10M** â Vybe wants âvibe codingâ to graduate from weekend prototypes to enterprise rollouts, bundling AI app-building with more security and deeper connections into corporate systems


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/cb66db49-134f-473a-ba9b-cad171d679c9/One_More_Thing.png?t=1770911096)

emails.subject: GTM Tool Spend by Growth Stage
emails.body:
View this post on the web at https://gtmvault.wiki/p/gtm-tool-spend-by-growth-stage

A founder told me something recently that perfectly captures what is quietly breaking inside modern go-to-market teams, particularly at early and mid-stage SaaS companies that are trying to look sophisticated before they are structurally ready.
They replaced five sales reps with tools.
A few months later, they hired three people whose full-time job was managing, debugging, and coordinating those tools.
Not because the tools were bad, and not because automation does not work, but because the company invested in GTM infrastructure years before it had earned the patterns, ownership boundaries, and operational discipline required to absorb that complexity without slowing itself down.
This is one of the most common GTM failures I see, and it almost always hides behind the language of best practices, modern stacks, and what serious companies are supposed to look like.
In reality, it is not a tooling problem at all.
It is a timing problem.
Premature tooling does not remove cost, it relocates it
GTM tooling rarely fails because of price. It fails because it fundamentally reshapes how cost shows up inside the organization.
When tools are introduced too early, cost shifts away from visible headcount and into invisible coordination overhead, where work that once lived inside a single personâs judgment is now fragmented across systems, enrichment rules, automations, dashboards, and handoffs that no one fully owns end to end.
Slack fills up with questions that should never have existed.
Automations quietly break and degrade trust in data.
Teams feel busy, instrumented, and operationally advanced, while learning velocity and execution speed quietly slow down.
This is the hidden tax of premature GTM tooling.
GTM tool spend is a stage decision, not a best practice
One of the most damaging habits in SaaS is copying the GTM stack of a $10M or $50M ARR company while operating at $300k or $500k ARR, under the assumption that tooling sophistication is what created their success.
At early stages, your constraint is not efficiency, scale, or coverage. It is signal. You are still learning who buys, why they buy, how they describe their pain, and what actually moves them to act. Any tool that increases distance from those signals, even if it saves time on paper, is a liability.
As companies scale, that constraint changes. Patterns stabilize. Volume introduces real friction. Only then does tooling begin to create leverage instead of noise.
GTM engineering is the discipline of aligning tool spend to that reality rather than to aspirational maturity.
Why early automation almost always backfires
Automation is usually justified as a way to save time, but early automation rarely removes work. Instead, it redistributes it across a wider surface area that requires constant attention to maintain.
Judgment that once lived in a founderâs head is now encoded into routing logic, enrichment rules, scoring thresholds, sequences, and brittle integrations between tools. When something breaks or behaves unexpectedly, there is no single owner who can diagnose the system holistically, so debugging replaces learning.
This is how teams end up automated but slower, instrumented but confused, and staffed with operators whose primary job is keeping GTM systems alive rather than driving revenue forward.
Automation without ownership does not scale execution.
It scales coordination.
Good GTM tooling removes delay, not thinking
The highest-performing GTM systems do not move faster because people think less. They move faster because idle time disappears from the execution path.
Waiting between steps.
Repeated qualification.
Multiple handoffs for the same decision.
These are the real killers of velocity.
Good tooling collapses execution so the same owner can observe a signal and act on it immediately, without waiting for alignment, approvals, or translation across functions. Bad tooling adds layers that require meetings before anything meaningful can happen.
The difference is not sophistication.
It is flow.
RevOps is the ownership boundary that makes complexity safe
As GTM complexity becomes unavoidable, the critical question shifts from whether to add tools to whether the organization has clear ownership over how those tools behave as a system.
RevOps earns its place when it functions as the system owner, not as a reporting layer or ticket queue, maintaining coherence as tooling increases and ensuring that every new layer reduces friction rather than introducing new failure points.
Without this ownership boundary, tools multiply faster than understanding.
With it, complexity compounds into leverage.
Automation only compounds once patterns exist
Automation is not inherently powerful. It is powerful only when applied to something stable.
Patterns in who converts.
Patterns in buyer behavior.
Patterns in what signals actually matter.
Before those patterns exist, automation accelerates noise. After they exist, it creates real scale.
The best teams delay automation longer than feels comfortable, then deploy it aggressively once the ground is solid. They earn the right to automate.
Early GTM optimizes for signal, not efficiency
Founder-led GTM still works at early stages because the objective is not efficiency, but learning speed.
Simple stacks, manual execution, and direct customer contact outperform sophisticated systems when clarity is still forming. Tools that reduce effort but also reduce contact with reality slow learning at the exact moment it matters most.
Efficiency comes later.
Signal comes first.
GTM engineering is capital discipline
GTM engineering is not about taste, trends, or assembling the right logos on a slide.
It is about allocating capital, attention, and complexity at the right moment in a companyâs lifecycle. The best teams are not the ones with the biggest stacks or the most automation, but the ones with the fewest coordination surfaces and the tightest feedback loops.
Tools do not replace people.
They replace waiting, rework, and misalignment, but only when the organization is ready.
If your GTM stack requires more people to manage than to execute, the problem is not tooling quality.
It is timing.

Unsubscribe https://substack..4DWe7qyylIJNSnBfenNYcmg-gEpExZ_BK1wVftVNoB0?

emails.subject: RE: Need some inspiration getting started with Pica? Well, I could use some detailed instructions on how to integrate with QuickBooks running on-premise...?
emails.body:
Hello Moe,

I am working on a custom SaaS app that needs to integrate with QuickBooks Enterprise that is installed on-site at our customer's office?

Our custom app would need to push data to either an Invoice or to an Estimate in QuickBooks running on a Desktop computer that is acting as the QuickBooks server.

Is that an integration your product can help enable?  If so, do you have any instructions or guidance on how to do?

Please let me know your thoughts.

Thank You!

Erol

Erol Eraybar
Principal Consultant | Strat-Tek, LLC
Office:  614.874.6257 | Email:  erol@StratTek.com<mailto:erol@StratTek.com>
www.StratTek.com<http://www.strattek.com/>

From: Moe Katib <moe@ship.picaos.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 8:24 PM
To: eroleraybar@gmail.com
Subject: Need some inspiration getting started with Pica?


Hi Erol Eraybar,

By now, you've hopefully started exploring what Pica can do. Today, we want to show you how other companies are using Pica to ship faster, automate smarter, and unlock real value.

For example, a current Pica client built an AI-powered personal assistant that is connected to G-Suite, Slack, and HubSpot, turning manual tasks into automated workflows.

Another client is using Pica to offer integrations to QuickBooks, Xero, and NetSuite for their customers to connect to those tools directly.

We're excited to see how you'll use Pica to make your products smarter and more efficient. If we haven't connected yet, I'd love to learn more about you and what you're building. Feel free to find some time here<https://c.vialoops.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcalendly.com%2Fjacob-picaos%2Fnew-meeting/1/0100019c549904d7-72be768b-4a67-4536-a395-9f0d1d6a8ec9-000000/JqtYE_msbc_FIZuMslA3oMo9ktv7SXDMPvxBZ_VIpuA=444>, or just let me know a time that works best for you.
Best,
Moe
Founder & CEO of Pica
You are receiving this email because you opted-in to receive updates from Pica
Pica, 650 California St floor 7, San Francisco, CA 94108
Unsubscribe <https://c.vialoops.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fapp.loops.so%2Funsubscribe%2Fcmlk7fft10ak801ky39fr55vy%2F0cdc86532fcab971eacb8b889ad56c02a2f1bd8936a6e1a0c262cd4538aeec0f455e4d3d1e5a6dd7f6b61c18df12254d7ca4741627c86c2f5ddd5e7f/1/0100019c549904d7-72be768b-4a67-4536-a395-9f0d1d6a8ec9-000000/0kG1mREg48Bla4CuactFqpkXaSe_BV2SATtjatIQgBM=444>

emails.subject: Medusa / Pica Intro
emails.body:
Sebastian, meet Moe Katib, Founder and CEO at Pica.

Moe, meet Sebastian Rindom, Founder and CEO at Medusa. Theyâve got some
customers that need to integrate with internal systems and I thought Pica
might be able to help.

I hope you enjoy connecting.

-Dan

emails.subject: OpenAI Ditches Nvidia + AI's $200M Political War + China's AI Blitz
emails.body:
Hi Moe!

This week in Silicon Valley, startups & tech:

* **OpenAI breaks up with Nvidia:** GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark runs on Cerebras chips, generating code 15x faster, marking a huge hit to Nvidia's inference monopoly

* **AI's $200M political arms race:** Anthropic drops $20M on a pro-regulation super PAC to counter OpenAI's $125M PAC ahead of the 2026 midterms

* **China launches an AI blitz:** MiniMax M2.5 delivers frontier intel at $0.30/1M tokens, ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 goes viral, and more

* **Waymo deploys next-gen robotaxis:** 6th-gen Driver cuts sensors 42%, costs under $20K per unit, and targets 1M+ weekly rides by year-end

* **xAI keeps losing co-founders:** Jimmy Ba and Tony Wu depart as Musk announces a reorg, while X quietly hits $1B ARR in subscriptions

###### **As we continue to build at**[ Context Ventures](https://www.contextvc.com/)**, we've launched**[ Startup Intros](https://startupintros.com/)** with a simple mission: to help early-stage founders find the right investors, faster and smarter.**

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

## ð¤ OpenAI Breaks Up with Nvidia: Codex Spark Runs on Cerebras

OpenAI just launched GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark, a lighter, faster version of its agentic coding tool. The headline number: 15x faster code generation. But the real story isn't speed. It's the chip underneath.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/f138b823-fd9f-4ed3-9912-8e358e73225a/image.png?t=1770950716)
Caption: [Introducing GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark | OpenAI](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-3-codex-spark/)

### ð The Cerebras Bet

Spark is OpenAI's first model to run on **Cerebras** hardware, not Nvidia. It's powered by the Wafer Scale Engine 3, a megachip with 4T transistors. OpenAI calls this the "first milestone" in a [$10B+ multi-year partnership](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/12/a-new-version-of-openais-codex-is-powered-by-a-new-dedicated-chip/) announced last month.

**Codex mirrors how devs work with two optimized modes:**

* **Spark (Rapid Prototyping)**: Quick iteration, low latency, and real-time collaboration run on Cerebras.

* **Full Codex (Deeper Reasoning)**: Multi-file refactors, complex system design, and code review across entire repos run on Nvidia.

**[Cerebras](https://www.cerebras.ai/press-release/cerebras-systems-raises-usd1-billion-series-h)**[ just raised $1B at a $23B valuation](https://www.cerebras.ai/press-release/cerebras-systems-raises-usd1-billion-series-h) and is pursuing an IPO. This isn't a science project. It's a real alternative to Nvidia's inference stack.

### ð What This Means for Nvidia

Training diversified years ago (Google TPUs, Amazon Trainium), but inference remained Nvidia's fortress. Spark cracks that wall. If Cerebras delivers lower latency at competitive cost for coding, the highest-volume AI workload, other labs will follow. Nvidia isn't threatened today (data center revenue hit $35B last quarter), but the monopoly narrative just got weaker.

**Translation:** OpenAI is hedging its compute future, and the first real alternative to Nvidia's inference dominance just shipped to production.

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

## ð³ï¸ AI's $200M Political Arms Race

Anthropic donated $20M to [Public First Action](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/anthropic-gives-20-million-to-group-pushing-for-ai-regulations-.html), a super PAC pushing for AI guardrails and transparency. The target: counter OpenAI-backed super PACs ahead of the 2026 midterms.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/d81de395-621f-4d4c-af65-0a78ac94acd5/image.png?t=1770952846)
Caption: 

### ð° AI Regulation Becomes a 2026 Election Issue

* **Public First Action (AI safety)**: Plans to raise $50M to $75M to back 30 to 50 candidates this cycle; led by former lawmakers Brad Carson and Chris Stewart.

* **Leading the Future (pro-AI)**: Has already raised $125M from a16z, OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman, Joe Lonsdale, Ron Conway, and Perplexity.

**Total AI PAC Spending**: over $200M committed for the midterms. For comparison, the entire crypto industry spent $135M on the 2024 elections. AI is already outspending crypto on political influence, and the cycle has just begun.

### ð¯ The Strategy: Bipartisan, Not Billionaire

Public First is backing candidates on both sides: Marsha Blackburn (kids' online safety) and Pete Ricketts (chip export limits). Their bet: [80% of Americans ](https://news.gallup.com/poll/694685/americans-prioritize-safety-data-security.aspx)[want AI safety rules,](https://news.gallup.com/poll/694685/americans-prioritize-safety-data-security.aspx)Â according to Gallup, is a stronger hand than billionaire donors. David Sacks has already criticized Anthropic for "regulatory capture."

**Why This Matters**: AI regulation shapes what you can build and how compliance costs look. The uncomfortable truth: the biggest companies actually benefit from regulation because they can afford the overhead. Smaller startups bear those costs disproportionately. The midterms will determine whether the US gets federal oversight or continues with a state-by-state patchwork.

**Translation:** The two biggest AI labs are now spending nine figures to shape the rules of their own industry, and the 2026 midterms will decide who wins.

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

### Ship the message as fast as you think

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/41ed07d6-b9bb-445a-99b4-4409300db482/Newsletters_Image_1920x1080__5_.png?t=1767982387)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wisprflow.ai/beehiiv/?utm_campaign=I4BDTTAXNH&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_term=founders_primary1&_bhiiv=opp_7be1df0c-e9b8-43c9-be40-87cb2270af20_1977f096&bhcl_id=e53f8df4-e8ab-4eb3-9a5d-4fa3d4a94db6_dc91c101-ecc3-44f2-a103-5a1689743ef9_f43c4f29-da91-4574-acfc-40c672e13111)
Caption: 

Founders spend too much time drafting the same kinds of messages. [Wispr Flow](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/beehiiv/?utm_campaign=I4BDTTAXNH&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_term=founders_primary1&_bhiiv=opp_7be1df0c-e9b8-43c9-be40-87cb2270af20_1977f096&bhcl_id=e53f8df4-e8ab-4eb3-9a5d-4fa3d4a94db6_dc91c101-ecc3-44f2-a103-5a1689743ef9_f43c4f29-da91-4574-acfc-40c672e13111) turns spoken thinking into final-draft writing so you can record investor updates, product briefs, and run-of-the-mill status notes by voice. Use saved snippets for recurring intros, insert calendar links by voice, and keep comms consistent across the team. It preserves your tone, fixes punctuation, and formats lists so you send confident messages fast. Works on Mac, Windows, and iPhone. Try [Wispr Flow for founders](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/beehiiv/?utm_campaign=I4BDTTAXNH&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_term=founders_primary1&_bhiiv=opp_7be1df0c-e9b8-43c9-be40-87cb2270af20_1977f096&bhcl_id=e53f8df4-e8ab-4eb3-9a5d-4fa3d4a94db6_dc91c101-ecc3-44f2-a103-5a1689743ef9_f43c4f29-da91-4574-acfc-40c672e13111).

[Start flowing free](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/beehiiv/?utm_campaign=I4BDTTAXNH&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_term=founders_primary1&_bhiiv=opp_7be1df0c-e9b8-43c9-be40-87cb2270af20_1977f096&bhcl_id=e53f8df4-e8ab-4eb3-9a5d-4fa3d4a94db6_dc91c101-ecc3-44f2-a103-5a1689743ef9_f43c4f29-da91-4574-acfc-40c672e13111)

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

## ð¨ð³ China's AI Blitz: Four Moves in One Week

While US labs fight over politics and chips, China shipped four major moves in a single week. The "catching up" narrative seems to be dying.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3615246a-6af0-430a-a831-dd4c05532dcf/image.png?t=1770953351)
Caption: [MiniMax M2.5: Built for Real-World Productivity.](https://www.minimax.io/news/minimax-m25)

### 
ð¸ MiniMax M2.5: The $0.30 Revolution

**MiniMax** launched [M2.5](https://www.minimax.io/news/minimax-m25) at $0.30 per million input tokens and $1.20 per million output tokens. The performance is shown by the 80.2% on SWE-Bench Verified, 37% faster than M2.1, matching Claude Opus 4.6 speed. It writes specs before code, decomposes features, and plans architecture unprompted.

### ð Alibaba's 120M Orders

**Alibaba** turned Qwen into an e-commerce engine. It took [120M+ orders](https://www.theinformation.com/briefings/alibabas-holiday-ai-shopping-campaign-generates-120-million-orders) in six days during the Lunar New Year with a $3.60 voucher. That's AI generating real commerce at scale, not a research demo. Western AI companies debate monetization while Alibaba prints transactions.

### ð¬ ByteDance Goes Viral

**ByteDance's** Seedance 2.0 video generation went viral in China, with state media calling it "bigger than Sora." The video generation race just got a serious new contender.

### ð [Z.ai](https://Z.ai)'s Demand Problem

**[Z.ai](https://Z.ai)** is [raising prices](https://www.reuters.com/technology/chinese-ai-startup-zhipu-hikes-prices-coding-plan-demand-rises-2026-02-12/) on GLM coding by 30%+ due to surging demand. When a Chinese AI company hikes prices instead of cutting them, demand outstrips supply even at rock-bottom prices.

**Translation:** China isn't just building competitive models; it's commercializing them faster, pricing them cheaper, and scaling them bigger than anyone expected.

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

## ð Autonomy Gets Real: Waymo + Applied Intuition

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/c8a59a5b-7dee-4148-ae13-800e866d9a22/image.png?t=1770953140)
Caption: [Waymo: Beginning fully autonomous operations with the 6th-generation Waymo Driver](https://waymo.com/blog/2026/02/ro-on-6th-gen-waymo-driver)

### ð§ Cheaper, Better, Weather-Ready

[Waymo dramatically overhauled its 6th-Gen Driver](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/waymo-begins-deploying-next-gen-ojai-robotaxis-to-extend-its-us-lead.html) on next-gen Ojai robotaxis:

* **Reduces Sensors**: Cameras from 29 to 13, lidars from 5 to 4, and radar to 6, to have 42% fewer sensors with greater resolution and range.

* **Reduces Costs**: Under $20K per unit, making it 50%+ cheaper than 5th-gen.

* **Climate-Adaptable**: Includes hydrophobic coatings and modular components that are swappable for different climates.

### ð 1M Waymo Rides by End of 2026

Waymo currently delivers 400K+ paid rides weekly across six cities and is targeting expansion to 1M+ by the end of the year.

* **Hyundai Supply**: Providing 50,000 IONIQ 5 units, the largest single order in autonomous driving history.

* **New Cities**: 20+ additional cities planned, including Tokyo and London.

**One Wrinkle**: GOP lawmakers flagged concerns about Chinese-made **Geely** vehicles in the fleet. Waymo says it provides zero access to autonomous tech or rider data.

**Translation:** Autonomous driving crossed from experiment to real business at real scale, and the unit economics finally work.

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

## ð Product Launches Worth Knowing

[Google Gemini 3 Deep Think](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-deep-think/): Updated to better solve science, research, and engineering challenges; expanding access via the Gemini API to select researchers.

[YouTube for Apple Vision Pro](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/02/12/youtube-app-apple-vision-pro/): Finally launched with standard, 180-degree, and 360-degree video plus Shorts. M5 chip devices play 8K.

[Samsung HBM4](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-12/samsung-says-it-starts-commercial-shipment-of-hbm4-to-customer-mlj2njno): First commercial shipments sent, racing SK Hynix and Micron for Nvidia supply; memory bandwidth is becoming the bottleneck in AI training and inference.

[Sony WF-1000XM6](https://www.sony.com/wf-1000xm6) Launched at $330 with AI-powered beamforming mics, improved ANC, and hands-free calling. Sony's flagship earbuds continue to set the bar.

[Nvidia GeForce Now on Fire TV](https://www.nvidia.com/geforce-now/) Native app on select Fire TV sticks, up to 1080p/60fps cloud gaming. Nvidia expands cloud gaming beyond PCs and mobile.

[Amazon Kiro](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/12/amazon-steers-teams-to-use-kiro-ai-coding-assistant.html) Internal messages reveal Amazon steering ~1,500 employees to its in-house AI coding assistant, drawing criticism from devs who prefer external tools.

[Z.ai](https://Z.ai)[ GLM Coding](https://www.reuters.com/technology/chinese-ai-startup-zhipu-hikes-prices-coding-plan-demand-rises-2026-02-12/) Hiking prices 30%+ for new subscribers to accommodate surging demand.

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

## ð Personnel Quick Hits: Who's Moving This Week?

[**xAI co-founders depart**](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/10/musks-xai-loses-second-co-founder-in-two-days-as-jimmy-ba-departs.html)**:** Jimmy Ba and Tony Wu exit this week, following Igor Babuschkin, Kyle Kosic, Christian Szegedy, and Greg Yang. Six of the original co-founders are now gone.

[**Musk announces xAI reorg**](https://www.theinformation.com/briefings/x-subscriptions-hit-1-billion-annualized-revenue-exec-says)**:** Restructuring "required parting ways with some people" to "improve speed of execution." Meanwhile, X quietly hit $1B ARR from $3-$40/month subscriptions.

**[DOJ antitrust chief Gail Slater forced out](https://www.nbcnews.com/business/business-news/justice-antitrust-chief-slater-leaving-rcna258759)****:** Left effective immediately after months of tensions with senior cabinet officials. Viewed as a win for tech giants over aggressive antitrust postures.

**[DOJ charges former L3Harris boss](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/11/doj-says-trenchant-boss-sold-exploits-to-russian-broker-capable-of-accessing-millions-of-computers-and-devices/)****:** Peter Williams pleaded guilty to stealing and selling eight hacking tools from Trenchant to a Russian broker with government ties.

**[Ben Thompson on agentic commerce](https://stratechery.com/)****:** In a Stratechery Q&A, Thompson argues AI agents could reshape how consumers buy products, threatening the current ad model.

**[OpenAI's chief economist on adoption](https://www.semafor.com/article/02/11/2026/ais-challenge-is-not-the-tech-but-the-user-openai-chief-economist)****:** Ronnie Chatterji says AI's biggest 2026 problem isn't models; it's getting companies to actually use them.

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

## ð Editor's Note

###### At[ Startup Intros](https://www.startupintros.com/), our mission is to bring the latest founder-investor news straight to your inbox, keeping you ahead in the fast-paced world of Silicon Valley.

### ð­ Parting Thoughts

This was one of those weeks where you could feel the tectonic plates shifting.

OpenAI shipped production inference on non-Nvidia chips. Anthropic and OpenAI are spending $200M+ to fight each other at the ballot box. China made frontier intel cost $0.30 per million tokens. And Waymo proved autonomous driving scales with cheaper, better hardware.

The common thread is that labs are competing at every layer: chips, models, policy, and deployment. For founders, more competition means more options, lower costs, and more leverage. So, the best time to build is always when the giants are distracted, fighting each other.

Forward to a friend and hit reply to let me know what you're seeing in your world.

Share the Newsletter (https://startupintros.beehiiv.com/subscribe/dc91c101-ecc3-44f2-a103-5a1689743ef9/referrals?_bhlid=1a0c30b1d1c828aa8b4c44d5069ae964f39996f9&jwt_token=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWJzY3JpYmVyX2lkIjoiZGM5MWMxMDEtZWNjMy00NGYyLWExMDMtNWExNjg5NzQzZWY5IiwicHVibGljYXRpb25faWQiOiI4MDcyNTZjZC0wYTljLTQ4ZTEtODIwOC1kZDU5MDliNTRmNzkiLCJhY2Nlc3NfdHlwZSI6InJlYWQtb25seSIsImV4cCI6MTc3MTEyNjg2NiwiaXNzIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9hcHAuYmVlaGlpdi5jb20iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzA5NTQwNjZ9.62n5LhJDif4AfqzGA_--HRFcTCSWNlgjqCfntO-tRcQ)

Till next time!

emails.subject: [FounderCoHo @Stanford Event] Startup Fundraising in 2026: Building the Capital Path
emails.body:
View this post on the web at https://foundercoho.substack.com/p/foundercoho-stanford-event-startup

Fundraising is taking longer, and unclear investor feedback is leaving many founders trapped in the âtoo early, come back laterâ loop.
The strongest founders donât wait for clarity; they design for the capital strategy from day one, and it fundamentally changes how investors evaluate the business.
In this practical session, we will cover:
What investors actually care about beyond your deck
How to embed exit strategy thinking from day one
The unseen link between fundraising and M&A readiness
Built for Stanford and Bay Area founders preparing for fundraising or exit, this is real, battle-tested insight from someone whoâs done it, not just theory.
Three founders; VC and M&A experts who actually cracked it will share their playbooks on Feb 23, 2026 at Stanford:
ð [Register here: https://luma.com/dnzgomnh [ https://substack.com/redirect/e3384297-c28c-497b-a891-560b398f4a39?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]]
Featured Speakers
âBrendan Marshall  [ https://substack.com/redirect/92207b55-1dde-4fa3-a497-0f40b2cc1ec7?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]â Managing Director @ Stanford-StartX | Ex-CEO & Cofounder @ Flow (acq.) | Forbes 30U30 | Stanford MBA | Venture Expert & 2X Founder. Brendan leads StartX, the Stanford-affiliated accelerator that has supported hundreds of founders from idea to growth. As a former CEO and founder himself, heâs navigated fundraising from both sides of the table, pitching investors, closing rounds, and now helping new founders do the same across stages and market cycles.
âDerek Yan  [ https://substack.com/redirect/c83c7fbc-7f0d-4191-91be-3fb52fa3ab7a?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]â Author of The Toughest Sell [ https://substack.com/redirect/3e587266-5726-4b07-8d2e-e6a38b0c8be8?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] | ex-CTO & Founder, Polarr (acq.) | Stanford Alumni. Derek Yan is Founder and former CTO of Polarr, which scaled to 100M+ devices and was acquired by Pixieset in May 2025. He raised $13.5M from Threshold, Partech, and the Stanford-StartX Fund. Drawing on 100+ investor and acquirer conversations, Derek authored The Toughest Sell, sharing practical insights on building fundable, exit-ready companies.
âModerated by Jing Conan Wang [ https://substack.com/redirect/6244548d-656f-4194-8969-cefed2e2fced?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ], Founder @ DeepVista AI & FounderCoHo (Ex-Google DeepMind).
What Weâll Explore
What Investors Actually Evaluate (And What They Donât Tell You) Beyond deck and metrics, learn what creates investor conviction. Derek shares readiness signals, proof of customer insight, and why founder conviction predicts fundraising success.
Building with Exit Optionality from Day One: How fundraising decisions, investor selection, cap table structure, and product positioning impact future options. Learn to balance growth ambitions with strategic flexibility for multiple exit paths.
The Fundraising-to-M&A Pipeline: Hidden Connections Drawing from 100+ conversations, Derek reveals overlaps between fundraising and exits. Learn how investor outreach translates to acquirer conversations and what makes companies acquirable.
Operating While You Raise (And After You Raise) Maintain product momentum and team velocity while fundraising. Derek shares tactical approaches to recognizing genuine investor interest and planning multiple scenarios before rushed decisions.
Youâll leave with mental models, concrete examples, and a framework you can immediately apply to your own fundraising and company-building journey.
Event Details:
Date: February 23, 2026 (Monday)
Time: 6:00 PM â 9:00 PM PT
Location: Stanford University, Stanford, CA (Details will be shared after registration)
RSVP: Spots are limited; registration is required to join this in-person session.
Event Schedule:
ââââââââââ6:00 PM - 6:30 PM: Networking and Dinner
ââââââââââ6:30 PM - 7:20 PM: Panel and Q&A
ââââââââââ7:20 PM onwards: Socializing and Continued Conversations
âPartners
âDaytona [ https://substack.com/redirect/accec41d-8232-49a2-b487-bdad621b7cb2?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] - Infrastructure for Massively Parallel RL Rollouts.
âAssociation of Chinese Students and Scholars at Stanford (ACSSS) - Founded in 1989, ACSSS is the largest nonprofit student organization at Stanford.
Meet your Host!
FounderCoHo is a community for founders to share their hard-earned wisdom and connect with fellow founders in their journey.
With 160,000+ subscribers across YouTube and Substack, weâve featured industry leaders, including Lyftâs Co-Founder, the inventor of CRISPR, and AlphaChipâs co-author, alongside many successful founders and senior executives.
We host monthly in-person founder meetups where members exchange insights and build meaningful connections. Explore more: https://foundercoho.com/ [ https://substack.com/redirect/5d01b18e-aaf2-4188-92eb-1dd2b49f1c06?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]
ð [Register here: https://luma.com/dnzgomnh [ https://substack.com/redirect/e3384297-c28c-497b-a891-560b398f4a39?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]]
Questions? Just hit reply.

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9mb3VuZGVyY29oby5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_.9P-iZOBrejXV1IWoH939qxda6ywmXCtRAmb2gplxkvk?

emails.subject: Re: Insight Partners Reaching Out
emails.body:
Hey Moe,

I figure you are heads down building, but wanted to reach out once more in case my previous note slipped by.

I'm definitely curious to learn more about Pica and would love to find a time to connect in the near future. If it makes more sense, I'm also happy to find a time later down the road when you may have some more availability.

I'm rooting for Pica and looking forward to having the chance to meet.

Best,
Aidan

On Feb 3, 2026 12:50 PM, "Aidan Waldman" <awaldman@insightpartners.com<mailto:awaldman@insightpartners.com>> wrote:
Hi Moe,

I hope all is well. I'm reaching out to follow up on my previous message in case it slipped through the cracks. If you are up to it, I'd love to find a time to meet.

I'm particularly excited to learn more about Pica and your vision first-hand as I've been following the company from afar for some time now.

Let me know if you have a quick slot for a 15-30 minute chat this week or next. If you are particularly busy, we can also schedule a connection further down the road. Looking forward to meeting soon.

Best,
Aidan

On Jan 28, 2026 3:35 PM, 'Aidan Waldman' <awaldman@insightpartners.com<mailto:awaldman@insightpartners.com>> wrote:
Hi Moe,

I hope you are doing well. Iâm a software investor at Insight Partners, an NYC-based venture and growth fund that has backed companies such as Wiz, Databricks, SentinelOne, and Hinge Health.

I'd love to find time for us to connect to learn more about each other and hear the story + vision for Pica - really excited to see that you are building both a knowledge base and agentic development platform.

Let me know your availability in the coming days or weeks to hop on a quick Zoom - happy to meet whenever is convenient. Wishing you and your team luck in building and growing.

Best,
Aidan

emails.subject: Women in AI Weekly｜Movement, Mindfulness & Community
emails.body:
View this post on the web at https://clairexie.substack.com/p/women-in-ai-weeklymovement-mindfulness

Hey friends,
February brings Valentineâs Dayâbut at Women in AI, we think self-love goes beyond flowers and chocolates.
It looks like showing up for your work, your body, and your community. Itâs choosing movement, reflection, and connection as fuel, especially as founders, operators, and builders navigating fast-moving systems and big responsibilities.
This week, weâre leaning into care from the inside out: taking time to recharge, building alongside women who get it, and creating space to grow together ð
Announcements
Weâre excited to launch a new partnership series with Equinoxâbringing movement, mindfulness, and meaningful connection into how we gather. Women in AI members will receive exclusive access to bi-monthly group classes, plus a complimentary 2-day Equinox pass for future use at any Bay Area location. This partnership is part of our commitment to building not just strong leaders in AI, but supported, energized ones.
Want access to benefits like this? Apply to join [ https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e76e679a-6cbf-4cdd-9e82-90990fcfcfd9?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] and lock in current pricingâmembership rates will be increasing soon.
Featured Events
Women in AI x Equinox: Private Cycling Class
Wednesday, February 18ï½6:30 PM - 8:00 PMï½San Francisco
Our members-only series kicks off with 45-minutes of high-energy cycling followed by a guided meditationâdesigned to help you reset, recharge, and reconnect. Arrive early or stay late to enjoy full access to Equinox amenities. Spots are limited for this intimate experienceâreserve yours now, or apply to become a member [ https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e76e679a-6cbf-4cdd-9e82-90990fcfcfd9?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] to access this and other exclusive events.
Women in AI x Gold House Ventures: Lunar New Year Gala | Asian Art Museum
Tuesday, February 24ï½6:00 PM - 9:00 PMï½San Francisco
Join us for a black-tie Lunar New Year Gala bringing together founders, operators, and investors in AI for an evening focused on connection, reflection, and community. This intimate gathering is designed for those actively building or backing companies who value thoughtful conversation and meaningful relationships.
Community Partner Events
Women in AI x Supermomos: AGI Royale Gala
Thursday, March 5ï½6:00 PM - 9:00 PMï½San Francisco
Step into an unforgettable evening where cutting-edge AI meets elevated celebration. Expect futuristic formal fits, artificially generated beats, and real conversations with founders, operators, investors, and leaders from top tech companies. From curated networking zones to game lounges, a live DJ, and themed experiences throughout the night, this is where the AI community comes to connect, celebrate, and build meaningful relationships. Dress to impress in your best futuristic glam.
Women in AI x Fintech Next: Fintech Forward
Tuesday, March 10ï½5:00 PM - 7:00 PMï½Downtown San Francisco
A forward-looking conversation on AI and emerging tech in fintechâcovering payments, platforms, and the infrastructure shaping the future of financial services. Executives, founders, investors, and senior leaders will share insights on where real opportunity is forming and how to position yourself to grow and lead. Seats are limited, and registration will be closing soonâdonât miss it.
Partner With Us!
If youâre a startup, VC, or service provider looking to connect with our community, weâd love to explore a partnership. Opportunities include:
Event Sponsorship â showcase your brand at curated Women in AI gatherings
Membership Perks Packaging â provide exclusive benefits or offers to our members
Newsletter Features â reach 50,000+ subscribers with your company, tool, or service
Community Partnerships â collaborate on programs, initiatives, or special projects
Interested in partnering? Connect with us to discuss the best fit for your brand.
Become A Member

Membership unlocks deeper engagement with the community, priority invites to programs, and a range of resources designed to help you learn, grow, and lead in AI.
Weâre Hiring!
Women in AI is becoming the global community where female builders, operators, and AI leaders come together to grow, create, and lead. As our ecosystem expands, weâre hiring across leadership and support rolesâOperations, Charity, Venture, Retreats, Events (NYC, LA, Austin), plus Social Media, Membership, and SF Eventsâif you want to grow your skills and help shape the future of Women in AI.
Support Women in AI
As a volunteer-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit, Women in AI relies on your support to host events, expand access to mentorship, and grow a community where women donât just participate in AIâthey shape it. Every contribution helps create more opportunities and connections for women building whatâs next.
Stay Connected
Keep up with Women in AI and connect with the community through our WhatsApp group [ https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/60c4ae4d-dad2-4c08-b97e-c1e60ea06e79?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ], LinkedIn [ https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/1f9e26a3-ef6f-4904-8c36-1e49306e8fbe?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ], Instagram [ https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/878ace6a-6de4-474c-ad0e-7573e0c53f5b?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ], and X [ https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/4da70ba4-18a4-4f40-813b-cb8ca4f15f85?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] for the latest programs, events, and opportunities.
