emails.subject: FriendliAI - The Inference Engine Behind vLLM
emails.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/440573d3-f114-4c50-a65f-14d8457ad674/DD2.png?t=1747020074)
Caption: 

## CV Deep Dive

**Today, weâre talking with **[Byung-Gon Chun (Gon)](https://www.linkedin.com/in/byung-gon-chun/)**, Founder and CEO of **[FriendliAI](https://friendli.ai)**.**

**If youâve used vLLM, youâve used technology that traces back to Gonâs research.** His lab at Seoul National University published the ORCA paper, which introduced Continuous Batching, a technique that fundamentally changed how LLM inference engines handle concurrent requests. ORCA directly inspired vLLM and has since become an industry standard across virtually every major serving framework. The team behind one of the most influential advances in modern inference infrastructure is now building the platform to make it all accessible.

**FriendliAI is a world-class inference platform that helps teams deploy and scale large open-source and custom AI models efficiently, reliably, and at significantly lower cost.** Built on a proprietary inference stack optimized across batching, quantization, scheduling, caching, and GPU kernels, Friendli positions itself as a premier alternative to closed model providers like OpenAI and Anthropic, offering comparable performance and convenience for the worldâs best open-source models without the proprietary markup. The platform supports serverless APIs, dedicated endpoints, and container-based deployments including fully on-prem setups, giving teams the flexibility to choose the right deployment without being locked into a single approach.

**Friendli was founded on a conviction that training was getting all the attention, but inference was quietly becoming the real bottleneck** where cost, latency, and reliability challenges compound as AI moves from research demos into production systems serving millions of users. Today, the platform serves AI-native startups, enterprises deploying LLMs and multimodal models at scale, and model providers seeking broader adoption without the burden of operating massive inference infrastructure. Customers use Friendli for chat applications, code generation, agent systems, multimodal AI, and internal enterprise copilots, often achieving 50%+ cost savings by switching workloads from closed APIs to Friendliâs optimized open-source stack.

**Notable partnerships include **[LG AI Research](https://friendli.ai/blog/lg-ai-research-partnership-exaone-4.0), where Friendli serves as the official inference partner for their EXAONE family of models, and **Twelve Labs**, whose industry-leading video understanding models rely on Friendliâs engine for compute-intensive video inference at scale. The company also recently launched a [Switch campaign](https://friendli.ai/switch) offering up to $50,000 in inference credits for teams ready to migrate off closed providers, a signal of their confidence in the economics.

In this conversation, Gon walks us through the founding story of FriendliAI, what sets their inference stack apart in an increasingly crowded landscape, and why he believes the era of the âconvenience taxâ is ending for teams ready to take control of their model infrastructure.

**Letâs dive in â¡ï¸**

**Read time: 8 mins**

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

## _Our Chat with Gon _ð¬

_**Gon, welcome to Cerebral Valley! Your research has shaped how most of the industry does inference today. Give us the backstory on you and Friendli.**_

**Iâm Byung-Gon Chun, people call me Gon, and I am the Founder and CEO of FriendliAI.** Before starting Friendli, I was a professor at Seoul National University working on large-scale machine learning systems, particularly around efficient inference and training of large AI models. A lot of our early work focused on how to make these cutting-edge models actually usable in production.

**One of our most well-known contributions is ORCA and Continuous Batching.** ORCA inspired vLLM and several other open-source frameworks, and Continuous Batching has become an industry standard for efficient LLM inference. That work gave us a front-row seat to just how painful the inference problem really was, even for well-resourced teams.

**Friendli really grew out of that frustration.** Teams were spending enormous amounts of time and money to serve these models fast and reliably. When we started the company, training was getting all the attention, but I knew that inference was becoming the real bottleneck. We started Friendli to solve that problem end-to-end and to make high-performance inference accessible without requiring every company to build its own infrastructure from scratch.

**It wasnât a single âeurekaâ moment, but rather a wave of realization.** When we published the ORCA paper at OSDI, we knew we had solved a fundamental inefficiency in how GPUs process requests. But seeing the open-source community, specifically projects like vLLM, adopt these ideas so rapidly was the real validation. It was surreal to watch a technique we developed in the lab become the default standard for the entire industry almost overnight.

**That transition from academic paper to industry standard was actually the spark for Friendli.** We realized that while the concept of continuous batching was out there, implementing it reliably at enterprise scale, with the necessary scheduling, caching, and redundancy, was a completely different beast. I saw brilliant teams struggling to build this infrastructure in-house, and it became clear that the world didnât need just another open-source library; they needed a robust, enterprise-grade platform that just worked.

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/friendliai/status/2019425836773064724)

_**You use the phrase âconvenience taxâ to describe what closed providers charge. Break that down for us.**_

**Friendli is a world-class AI inference platform that helps teams deploy and scale large open-source and custom AI models efficiently, reliably, and at much lower cost.** We see ourselves as a premier alternative to closed model providers like OpenAI and Anthropic. Those providers offer high-quality models, but they essentially charge a convenience tax through their proprietary APIs. Friendli provides the same level of convenience for the worldâs best open-source models without that markup.

**For developers, that means using state-of-the-art open-source models via a familiar API with low latency and high throughput.** For AI teams, it means they donât need to worry about GPU utilization, kernel optimization, or auto-scaling and operations. We handle that under the hood so they can focus on building their AI products, not managing inference infrastructure.

_**Whoâs getting the most value from Friendli today? Paint us a picture of your typical customer.**_

**Our core users fall into three groups: AI-native startups, enterprises deploying LLMs or multimodal models at scale, and model providers who want broader adoption without running massive inference operations.** The teams seeing the most value are those operating at scale, where moving away from closed model ecosystems to optimized open-source inference directly improves product quality and their bottom line.

**A great example is actually happening right now in the developer tools space.** Weâre seeing a huge surge in usage from thousands of individual developers and popular coding agents, often accessing us through platforms like OpenRouter, where we often win due to our speed and high reliability.

**Previously, many of these developers felt locked into expensive closed models like Claude for their coding tasks because they believed open-source alternatives couldnât compete.** But that gap has closed dramatically. Models like GLM, MiniMax, DeepSeek, and Kimi have gotten extremely good at code generation and reasoning.

**Weâve seen developers switch their agent backends from Claude to running these high-performance open-source models on Friendli.** The feedback is consistent: they are getting comparable coding proficiency, but at a price point that makes their unit economics actually work. For a coding agent that might make hundreds of inference calls to refactor a single codebase, that cost difference is the difference between a viable product and burning cash. They come for the savings, but they stay because the latency on our engine makes the âfeels-likeâ performance of their agents significantly snappier.

_**Talk to us about some existing use cases. What kinds of workloads are people running on Friendli?**_

**Friendli is used for chat applications, code generation, agent systems, multimodal AI, and internal enterprise copilots.** Many customers come to us after hitting scaling limits and experiencing challenges such as unpredictable latency, inefficient GPU usage, or rising costs. By switching from closed APIs to Friendli-powered open-source models like Qwen, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, or Llama, they often see significant latency improvements and much better cost efficiency without changing their application logic.

**Weâre also working with several leading coding agents, helping them deploy and scale both open-source and custom fine-tuned models.** These agents require low latency and high throughput to provide a seamless user experience, and our platform allows them to achieve that while maintaining total control over their model stack.

**Another exciting partnership is with Twelve Labs, who use Friendli to run inference for their industry-leading video understanding models.** Video inference is incredibly compute-intensive, and our engine ensures they can deliver fast, accurate insights from video content at scale. Itâs a great example of how our stack handles workloads that go well beyond standard text-based LLM serving.

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/friendliai/status/1995565792474399223)

_**For a team thatâs curious but hasnât tried Friendli yet, how fast can they get something running?**_

**Itâs incredibly easy.** Most customers get started by selecting a model and redirecting their existing OpenAI-compatible API calls to Friendli. Itâs essentially a drop-in replacement with the same API format and dramatically better economics.

**One of our most powerful features is our **[one-click deployment for over 500,000 open-source models](https://www.friendli.ai/models)** from Hugging Face.** Whether you need serverless APIs, dedicated endpoints, or container capabilities for on-prem needs, you can move into production within minutes. We recommend starting with latency-sensitive workloads, high-throughput inference, or long-context reasoning use cases to see the platformâs strengths firsthand.

_**Tell us about the LG AI Research partnership. Thatâs a unique position for an inference company to be in.**_

**Our partnership with **[LG AI Research](https://friendli.ai/blog/lg-ai-research-partnership-exaone-4.0)** is a great example of how we empower model providers to reach developers at scale.** LG is building incredible models like K-EXAONE, which is a massive 236 billion parameter hyper-attention mixture-of-experts model. Friendli serves as the official inference partner, providing both serverless and dedicated endpoints for the EXAONE model family.

**This allows developers to access state-of-the-art Korean and English language capabilities with the performance and reliability of the Friendli inference stack.** By handling the complex infrastructure required for a model of that scale, we are helping LG reach a much broader audience of developers who can now integrate EXAONE into their applications with just a few lines of code. For model providers, thatâs the value proposition in a nutshell: we take the serving burden off their plate so they can focus on model development and community.

_**How are you measuring impact for your customers? What does success look like in hard numbers?**_

**We focus on latency, throughput, reliability, and crucially, cost per token.** By moving workloads from closed models to our optimized open-source stack, our customers often achieve cost savings of 50% or more. Thatâs not a marginal improvement. For teams running inference at scale, it fundamentally changes their unit economics.

**Ultimately, success means customers can scale their AI products and usage without scaling their operational complexity or budget at the same rate.** Thatâs the bar we hold ourselves to.

_**You recently launched a Switch campaign with some serious incentives. Whatâs behind that, and who should be paying attention?**_

**We actually just launched the **[Switch campaign](https://friendli.ai/switch)** specifically because we know the main barrier to moving off closed APIs isnât just technical, itâs the risk of transition.** Teams have built their applications around a specific providerâs API, and even when the economics donât make sense anymore, the switching cost feels high.

**To help with that, weâre offering up to $50,000 in inference credits for eligible teams that switch their workloads to Friendli.** Itâs designed to give companies the runway they need to optimize their implementation and see the 50%+ cost savings for themselves, without any upfront financial risk. If youâre hitting scale and the convenience tax is starting to hurt, this is the time to make the move.

_**The inference space is getting crowded. Whatâs your honest take on what makes Friendli different from the other players?**_

**Friendli is built on a proprietary inference stack, highly optimized across batching, quantization, scheduling, caching, and GPU kernels.** Our architecture is flexible and decoupled, supporting serverless and dedicated endpoints as well as container-based deployments including on-prem and without forcing a single deployment model. That matters because different customers have very different requirements around data residency, security, and infrastructure preferences.

**This flexibility, combined with our world-class performance on both custom and open-source models, makes us a true infrastructure partner, not a model provider.** Weâre not asking you to adopt our model ecosystem, we are making the models you choose run better, faster, and cheaper.

**The biggest misconception is fixating solely on the âsticker priceâ per million tokens.** Teams often look at a pricing page and choose the cheapest option, assuming all tokens are created equal. They arenât.

**What matters in production is performance stability.** You might find a provider that is cheaper on paper, but if their latency spikes during your peak traffic hours, or if their throughput degrades when you scale, you lose users. The âconvenience taxâ isnât just about money; itâs about the hidden cost of unreliable infrastructure. We tell teams to measure âperformance per dollarâ under load, not just the price of a token in a vacuum. Thatâs usually where the decision becomes obvious.

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/friendliai/status/2018911459619803230)

_**Take us under the hood. Why build your own engine from scratch instead of building on top of existing open-source frameworks?**_

**Friendli vertically optimizes AI model execution and infrastructure management end-to-end for inference.** We started with our own engine from the ground up, highly optimized for fast inference to avoid framework overhead, and built in extensive redundancy for reliable operation. We didnât want to layer on top of existing open-source serving frameworks, what we wanted was full control over every layer of the stack so we could push performance beyond whatâs possible with off-the-shelf solutions.

**This enables consistent performance across all deployment modes.** The system adapts in real time to dynamic inference workflows, ensuring that even under heavy load, the user experience remains fast and responsive. Whether youâre running a single model or orchestrating across multiple, the engine handles the complexity under the hood.

_**Whatâs been the hardest engineering problem youâve had to solve?**_

**The hardest challenge has been optimizing latency, throughput, and cost simultaneously across diverse real-world models and workflows.** Itâs common to optimize for one dimension at the expense of another. You can get low latency if you throw enough compute at it, or you can get great throughput if youâre willing to batch aggressively and accept higher latency. Doing all three at once, across a huge variety of model architectures, input patterns, and scale requirements, is where the real engineering difficulty lives.

**Achieving this required deep work across system runtimes, GPU kernels, and large-scale distributed infrastructure, with constant validation in production.** The research background of our team has been critical here. The same rigor that went into ORCA and Continuous Batching carries through to everything we build on the platform side.

_**What should people expect from Friendli over the next 6-12 months? Where are you headed?**_

**Weâre focused on unlocking higher-speed inference on GPUs beyond the limits typically seen with other service providers, while expanding high-speed inference for multimodal models and strengthening our self-serve platform.** We want to make it even easier for teams to escape the closed model trap by providing the fastest, most reliable way to run any custom or open-source model.

**We are also seeing a massive shift toward agentic workloads.** When you move from simple chatbots to agentic systems and reasoning models, the inference profile changes completely. An agent might need to âthinkâ for several steps, call tools, and iterate before answering the user.

**In a chat app, a 200-millisecond delay is annoying. In an agent loop that runs ten internal steps to solve one problem, that delay compounds into seconds of dead air.** Low latency becomes non-negotiable. We are seeing customers specifically asking for optimizations around low response latency, fast token generation, and efficient context caching because their agents are reading massive amounts of data and iterating on it in real-time. Friendliâs engine is uniquely suited for these agentic workloads because we optimize the entire execution path, not just the batching.

_**Lastly, tell us about the team. Whatâs the culture like, and what kind of people thrive at Friendli?**_

**Weâre a deeply technical, execution-driven team with strong roots in research and engineering.** Weâre [actively hiring](https://friendli.ai/careers) and looking for top talent in both the San Francisco Bay Area and in Korea across the company. We value people who are genuinely enthusiastic about the field, take initiative, and enjoy collaborating with others. Technical curiosity and a strong sense of ownership shape how we work and how we build the systems our customers rely on.

**Inference is becoming the backbone of AI products.** The teams that win wonât only have the best models but theyâll also have the best systems to run them, especially as open-source models continue to advance and reach parity with closed alternatives. Thatâs the problem weâre obsessed with at Friendli: world-class infrastructure that gives developers freedom to innovate.

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

Stay up to date on the latest with **FriendliAI**, follow them [here](https://friendli.ai/).

Read our past few **Deep Dives** below: 

* [**Join our Deep Dive series in 2026!**](https://cerebralvalley.beehiiv.com/p/join-our-deep-dive-series-in-2026)

* [**Redis is much more than a database ð¢**](https://cerebralvalley.beehiiv.com/p/redis-is-much-more-than-a-database)

* [**OpenAIâs Codex Lets One Engineer Ship Like a Team of Ten â¡**](https://cerebralvalley.beehiiv.com/p/openai-s-codex-lets-one-engineer-ship-like-a-team-of-ten)

* [**Tembo: The Background Coding Agents Company**](https://cerebralvalley.beehiiv.com/p/tembo-the-background-coding-agents-company)

**If you would like us to âDeep Diveâ a founder, team or product launch**, please reply to this email (_[newsletter@cerebralvalley.ai](mailto:newsletter@cerebralvalley.ai)__)_ or DM us on** **_[Twitter](https://twitter.com/cerebral_valley)_ or _[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cerebralvalley)_.

emails.body:

# **From Struggling to Scaling: The Secret to Product-Market Fit**

Youtube: From Struggling to Scaling: The Secret to Product-Market Fit | Ryan Marien (https://youtube.com/shorts/OeME7GKfUb4)



If you're building in Canada and wondering whether YC is worth it, you need to watch this one.



**[Ryan Marien](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryanmarien/?originalSubdomain=ca)** (Co-Founder, **[BioRender](https://www.biorender.com/)**, YC W18) shares why Bay Area companies ship faster, and what it takes for Canadian founders to compete globally. No sugar-coating. Just the real insights most people won't tell you.



He breaks down how BioRender navigated YC as a SaaS company, built momentum through disciplined execution, and scaled without losing their identity. This is the kind of talk founders bookmark, rewatch with their co-founders, and reference when making hard decisions about where to build and how to scale.



If you watch one TechTO talk this week, make it this one.



**Watch the full conversation on our YouTube **[**@TechTOorg**](https://www.youtube.com/@TechToorg)** - **==**and tell us in the comments: are you applying to YC, or building from Canada?**==



Youtube: What YC Doesnât Tell Canadian Founders | Ryan Marien (BioRender, YC W18) (https://youtu.be/RfbmvyBTgWc)

**WATCH NOW** (https://www.youtube.com/@TechToorg)







âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

Caption: 

### 

On **Thursday, February 19th**, we're bringing back **Together Vancouver**. This is the kind of evening that reminds us why we do what we do.



â**You'll hear from:**

[**Chris Neumann**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ckneumann/) - 4x founder, former GP at **[Panache Ventures](https://www.panache.vc/)**, helping founders crack the Silicon Valley code.
[**Scott Elliot**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/scott-elliot/) - CEO of [**Slate**](https://www.tryslatehq.com/), building the embedded lending infrastructure changing how platforms think about financing.
[**Sina Afrooze**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sinaafrooze/) - CEO of [**Apera AI**](https://apera.ai/), whose vision tech helps factory robots see and adapt like humans - trusted by six of the world's top automotive companies.
[**Simon Wong**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simon-wong-47072417/) - CEO of **[Pixieset](https://pixieset.com/)**, serving over 1 million photographers worldwide.â â 



Whether you're wrestling with your first hire, figuring out product-market fit, or just trying to find your people in Vancouver's tech scene, this is your night.



â**Event Details:**
**Thursday, February 19 | 6:00-9:00 PM PST**
SFU VentureLabs, 555 W Hastings St (11th Floor), Vancouverâ â ==_**Early bird sold out. General admission still available.**_==

Together Vancouver | Feb 19: (https://luma.com/together-vancouver-feb-19-2026)

**GET YOUR TICKET** (https://luma.com/together-vancouver-feb-19-2026)



âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

# _**NEXT BIG GIGS**_

A few roles across Canadian tech worth your attention this week:


[**Tech Financial Advisor at MiWorld Technologies**](https://jobs.techto.org/jobs/246993203-tech-financial-advisor) (Remote/Toronto, Contract)
An eCommerce startup needs help revising their business plan and building financial projections for investors. You'll create the financial model from agreed assumptions. Great for a finance professional with tech credentials who can turn strategy into investor-ready numbers.

[**Senior Front-End Specialist at Quartermaster Inc.**](https://jobs.techto.org/jobs/246517583-senior-front-end-specialist) (Toronto/Hybrid, $150k-$175k)
Build Toronto's home management platform connecting homeowners with local Pros. You'll own UI implementations across web and mobile using React and React Native. Lead the design system and mentor junior engineers. Perfect for someone with 6+ years of front-end experience who wants to solve hard problems for real neighborhood impact.

[**Events Operations Manager at TechTO**](https://jobs.techto.org/jobs/188748731-events-operations-manager) (Toronto, Full-time)
Own the planning and execution of 70+ annual events for Canada's tech community. You'll manage logistics, vendors, sponsors, and on-site coordination. Everything from monthly meetups to quarterly dinners to the annual retreat. Built for someone with 3-5 years of event experience who thrives under pressure and wants to shape Canada's tech ecosystem.







_Looking to hire or get hired? Check out the _**[_TechTO Job Board_](https://jobs.techto.org/jobs)**_ to see 1,000+ opportunities and learn how to get your companies jobs featured._

TechTO Job Board: (https://jobs.techto.org/jobs)







âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

Follow image link: (https://www.techto.org/events)
Caption: 



â==**Together Vancouver:**== Thursday, **February 19 @ 6:00pm (PT) - **[**GET YOUR TICKETS**](https://luma.com/together-vancouver-feb-19-2026)




==**Health Toronto 2026:**== Wednesday, **February 24 @ 6:00pm - **[**GET YOUR TICKETS**](https://luma.com/health-toronto-feb-24-2026)




==**NuSession:**====_** **_====**Engineering at Scale for 127M+ Customers:**==** **Wednesday, **February 24 @ 6:00pm - **[**APPLY NOW**](https://luma.com/nusession-february-24)




==**Together Toronto:**== Monday, **March 2 **@ **6:00pm - **[**GET YOUR TICKETS**](https://luma.com/together-toronto-mar-2-2026)â â 

_Donât forget to lock in your year of TechTO! _**[_Subscribe_](https://lu.ma/TechTO-Events)**_ to our full events calendar_

** FULL EVENTS CALENDAR** (https://lu.ma/TechTO-Events)





### ð¤** Know Someone Who Should Be on the TechTO Stage?**

Weâre always looking to spotlight founders, operators, and builders with powerful stories; the kind that inspire, teach, and move the Canadian tech community forward.

If you know someone whose journey deserves the mic - from early stumbles to breakout momentum - we want to hear from you.



**NOMINATE A SPEAKER** (https://form.typeform.com/to/B3MDCj8f)

_Help us bring fresh voices and new perspectives to future TechTO events and podcasts._



# 



âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

Follow image link: (https://www.techto.org/memberships)
Caption: 



If youâre looking for more ways to connect with the tech world, our **[TechTO Membership](https://www.techto.org/memberships)** is a great way to interact with others building in the space. 



Our membership program already supports hundreds of entrepreneurs and professionals building the next best companies in Canadian tech. Take advantage of exclusive members-only events, and opportunities to meet those who will inspire your next big idea. Learn more about our **[Membership options here](https://www.techto.org/memberships)**.

emails.subject: Building from Canada? Watch This First
emails.body:
----------
**You are receiving the Community Edition of todayâs newsletter - **_**[upgrade to a Member](https://www.techto.org/memberships)**_** to receive additional perks and experiences**


----------âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

# **From Struggling to Scaling: The Secret to Product-Market Fit**

Youtube: From Struggling to Scaling: The Secret to Product-Market Fit | Ryan Marien (https://youtube.com/shorts/OeME7GKfUb4)



If you're building in Canada and wondering whether YC is worth it, you need to watch this one.



**[Ryan Marien](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryanmarien/?originalSubdomain=ca)** (Co-Founder, **[BioRender](https://www.biorender.com/)**, YC W18) shares why Bay Area companies ship faster, and what it takes for Canadian founders to compete globally. No sugar-coating. Just the real insights most people won't tell you.



He breaks down how BioRender navigated YC as a SaaS company, built momentum through disciplined execution, and scaled without losing their identity. This is the kind of talk founders bookmark, rewatch with their co-founders, and reference when making hard decisions about where to build and how to scale.



If you watch one TechTO talk this week, make it this one.



**Watch the full conversation on our YouTube **[**@TechTOorg**](https://www.youtube.com/@TechToorg)** - **==**and tell us in the comments: are you applying to YC, or building from Canada?**==



Youtube: What YC Doesnât Tell Canadian Founders | Ryan Marien (BioRender, YC W18) (https://youtu.be/RfbmvyBTgWc)

**WATCH NOW** (https://www.youtube.com/@TechToorg)

emails.body:
Hi Ryan,

Great to connect as well â thanks for sharing those times.

You can reach me at +1 437 324 5556. I'm free for a quick call today anytime after 3pm PST (6pm EST).

Looking forward to connecting.

Best,

Moe

â

---
â

Moe Katib,
âFounder & CEO
âPica | The reliability layer for AI
â---

Hey Matt - thanks for the introduction and context.


â


â

Moe - great to connect, would be great to touch base this week and get your perspective. I have 4-6pm EST tomorrow or 1-2pm Friday. Let me know if any times work for you.


â

Ryan


â

(
https://9d62fee9.click.convertkit-mail2.com/e5un8l08mwc7hl67063h8h8ooeo22c5pgxvrndxwl287vng50dw6vmr3r6qzovo0llk926q4lg75egndpm47w32k2x9qw53qe8or3555kzr47pp4wnon7vxw084hg8mk4/3ohphdu302rxl4tp/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGVtYW5kY3VydmUuY29t
)

**************************
The Growth Newsletter #312
**************************

How to win on Reddit in 2026

Quick intermission from the experimentation series.

Today we're doing something we haven't done in a while â passing
the mic to someone else.

Colin James Belyea was a growth strategist at Demand Curve years
ago. He went on to build Karmic, a Reddit-focused agency that
helps brands build real organic influence on the platform. He
knows Reddit better than anyone I've talked to, and today he's
sharing their playbook.

This is the start of something I'm excited about: bringing in
folks operating at the frontier of growth & GTM so they can share
their insights with the DC community.

If you're one of those people (or know someone who is), just
reply to this email. We'd love to hear from you.

â Justin

(
https://9d62fee9.click.convertkit-mail2.com/e5un8l08mwc7hl67063h8h8ooeo22c5pgxvrndxwl287vng50dw6vmr3r6qzovo0llk926q4lg75egndpm47w32k2x9qw53qe8or3555kzr47pp4wnon7vxw084hg8mk4/n2hohquvmxg2prs0/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYWlyb3BzLmNvbS9sYXNoYXktbGV3aXMteC1haXJvcHMtd2ViaW5hci02MC1kYXlzLWJvZnUtcGxheWJvb2s_dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU5BX2VuX0JyYW5kX01PRl9CQV9MYXNoYXktTGV3aXMtNjBELUJPRlUtUGxheWJvb2tfUTEtMjAyNiZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPXBhaWQtbmV3c2xldHRlciZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlbWFuZGN1cnZl
)
Brought to you by AirOps (
https://9d62fee9.click.convertkit-mail2.com/e5un8l08mwc7hl67063h8h8ooeo22c5pgxvrndxwl287vng50dw6vmr3r6qzovo0llk926q4lg75egndpm47w32k2x9qw53qe8or3555kzr47pp4wnon7vxw084hg8mk4/n2hohquvmxg2prs0/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYWlyb3BzLmNvbS9sYXNoYXktbGV3aXMteC1haXJvcHMtd2ViaW5hci02MC1kYXlzLWJvZnUtcGxheWJvb2s_dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU5BX2VuX0JyYW5kX01PRl9CQV9MYXNoYXktTGV3aXMtNjBELUJPRlUtUGxheWJvb2tfUTEtMjAyNiZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPXBhaWQtbmV3c2xldHRlciZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlbWFuZGN1cnZl
)â

BOFU content no longer wins by existing on your site. It wins by
showing up in AI answers when buyers are making decisions.

In this session, Lashay Lewis from BOFU.ai breaks down how B2B
SaaS teams are capturing high-intent visibility in ChatGPT and
Perplexity. You will learn why competitors are winning âbest
[category]â queries, which BOFU page types LLMs surface and cite,
and how to turn AI visibility into measurable pipeline and
revenue.

The session focuses on practical frameworks, real customer
language, and a clear 60-day sprint your team can execute
immediately.

ð February 25, 2026
â° 12pm ET / 9am PT
ð Live on Zoom (recording available)

ð REGISTER NOW (
https://9d62fee9.click.convertkit-mail2.com/e5un8l08mwc7hl67063h8h8ooeo22c5pgxvrndxwl287vng50dw6vmr3r6qzovo0llk926q4lg75egndpm47w32k2x9qw53qe8or3555kzr47pp4wnon7vxw084hg8mk4/n2hohquvmxg2prs0/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYWlyb3BzLmNvbS9sYXNoYXktbGV3aXMteC1haXJvcHMtd2ViaW5hci02MC1kYXlzLWJvZnUtcGxheWJvb2s_dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU5BX2VuX0JyYW5kX01PRl9CQV9MYXNoYXktTGV3aXMtNjBELUJPRlUtUGxheWJvb2tfUTEtMjAyNiZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPXBhaWQtbmV3c2xldHRlciZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlbWFuZGN1cnZl
)â

Want to get in front of 103,000+ founders and marketers? Let's
chat (
https://9d62fee9.click.convertkit-mail2.com/e5un8l08mwc7hl67063h8h8ooeo22c5pgxvrndxwl287vng50dw6vmr3r6qzovo0llk926q4lg75egndpm47w32k2x9qw53qe8or3555kzr47pp4wnon7vxw084hg8mk4/48hvh7umvp9wekcq/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGVtYW5kY3VydmUuY29tL3Nwb25zb3JzaGlwcz91dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9Z3Jvd3RoLW5ld3NsZXR0ZXI=
).

********************************
Most brands get Reddit backwards
********************************

Insight from Colin James Belyea (
https://9d62fee9.click.convertkit-mail2.com/e5un8l08mwc7hl67063h8h8ooeo22c5pgxvrndxwl287vng50dw6vmr3r6qzovo0llk926q4lg75egndpm47w32k2x9qw53qe8or3555kzr47pp4wnon7vxw084hg8mk4/wnh2h6uqnk4mpmcl/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubGlua2VkaW4uY29tL2luL2NvbGluamFtZXNiZWx5ZWEv
)â Co-Founder at Karmic Reddit Agency (
https://9d62fee9.click.convertkit-mail2.com/e5un8l08mwc7hl67063h8h8ooeo22c5pgxvrndxwl287vng50dw6vmr3r6qzovo0llk926q4lg75egndpm47w32k2x9qw53qe8or3555kzr47pp4wnon7vxw084hg8mk4/reh8h9um437kr4a6/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2l0aGthcm1pYy5jb20vP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVtYW5kY3VydmU=
)â

Every other social platform rewards you for talking about
yourself until people care.

Reddit is the opposite. If you show up promoting your product,
Reddit will quietly make sure no one hears you.

Sometimes that throttling is obvious, like a removed post. More
often, it is subtle â your content stays up, but your
distribution disappears. Engagement stalls. It feels like you are
âposting consistently,â but you are not building reach.

â
It took us a long time (and more than one burned approach) to
understand what was happening. The lesson is simple, but
unintuitive:

On Reddit, the fastest way to gain influence is to stop trying to
promote yourself at all.

----------------------------------------------
From shadow accounts to trust and transparency
----------------------------------------------

When we first started working on Reddit, we avoided branded
accounts. We used anonymous profiles that looked like ordinary
users, and when a Thread was relevant to a client, we would
mention them casually.

At the time, it seemed like the only way to survive on a platform
known for punishing self-promotion. And for a while, it worked.

â
Then the FTCâs updated rule banning fake reviews and testimonials
(
https://9d62fee9.click.convertkit-mail2.
) landed. Suddenly, it was obvious this approach had no future.
Anything built on grey-hat methods was not going to compound. The
moment enforcement or platform norms shift, the entire investment
can evaporate.

So we scrapped it and committed to doing Reddit transparently.

Today, most teams we speak with understand the era of covert
Reddit marketing is over. But while transparency is necessary,
it's still not sufficient to make Reddit work for businesses.

-------------------------
Status comes before reach
-------------------------

Redditâs defenses are set up to protect it from accounts with
ulterior motives. If itâs obvious youâre there to shill or
manipulate, theyâll figure you out pretty quickly.

In this environment, influence and reach can only be unlocked
over time by building community trust first.

Every account starts with no status. Young, low-karma accounts
are watched more closely and throttled more aggressively.
Meanwhile, accounts that consistently contribute value earn
higher status over timeâtheir content ranks higher, and theyâre
trusted more and given more latitude.

To maximize reach, you have to operate within the bounds allowed
to an account at your current status level while deliberately
building up that status so more powerful actions become available
later.

Your account status is determined by two things:

* Account Karma (how the community responds to your content)
* Thread and Comment history (how moderators evaluate you)

Every interaction contributes to a running judgment: Is this
account giving more value than itâs taking? Only accounts that
consistently provide value will raise their status over time.

Weâve compiled our learnings on how to do this into a process we
recommend for any brand building an organic Reddit presence. We
call it the Karma Ladder.

â

-------------------------
Climbing the Karma Ladder
-------------------------

The Karma Ladder is a progression toward higher-impact Reddit
activity over time, while protecting your account and content
from platform risk.

Each step aims to increase reach while managing risk. The key is
to operate within the bounds allowed to your account at its
current status level, while deliberately building up that status
so more powerful actions become available later. Each step only
works if the previous step was executed properly.

â
Hereâs how it works:

Step 1: Laying the Foundation
-----------------------------

This phase is about looking and behaving like a real human.

Spend 30 days in subreddits completely unrelated to your
businessâplaces like r/coffee, r/books, or hobby communities.
Participate in conversations and build your account Karma. The
only goal is to establish baseline trust and safety with Redditâs
systems.

Most teams try to skip this step. Thatâs a mistake. Your account
needs this foundation before it can effectively reach your target
audience.

Advancement criteria:
ââ 30 days + 100 Karma

Sounds silly, but pet Subreddits are great for this

Step 2: Building Authority
--------------------------

Now you can start participating in Threads relevant to your
target customers in real time, but only through Comments.

Donât include links or mention yourself, but focus on adding
value. Answer questions thoughtfully, share insights, and
contribute to discussions without any promotional angle.

Your handle quietly associates your brand with the topic at hand
while your content builds authority. This is where the real work
happens (and where most competitors give up).

Advancement criteria:
ââ 60 days + 60 relevant comments

You wonât get a huge amount of upvotes, but you donât need to.

Step 3: Creating Engagement
---------------------------

Only once authority is established do you begin posting your own
Threads. Even then, start with low-risk conversation starters
designed to get the right people talking and reach as many of
them as possible.

Done well, these Threads can spread widely and position your
account as a curator of the most important discussions in the
space. This phase also builds critical credibility with
moderators, which will matter significantly when you reach Step
4.

This is often the hardest step. Getting 5-10 Threads to go viral
takes skill, timing, and sometimes a bit of luck. Donât rush
itâquality matters more than speed here.

Advancement criteria:
ââ 5-10 viral Threads (100k+ impressions for consumer, 25k+ for
B2B)

This one produced 366k impressions in 48 hours

Step 4: Direct Intent
---------------------

At this point, youâve earned the ability to selectively direct
attention. Even still, youâll want to manage risk appropriately.

By now, you should have 500+ Karma and meaningful relationships
with Subreddit moderators. Before you post anything promotional,
reach out to moderators directly. Share what youâre thinking
about posting and ask for their input. Most will appreciate the
courtesy and give you guidance on how to do it in a way that
benefits the community.

Think of this as insurance for the riskier swings youâre about to
take.

Start by adapting existing thought leadership into a Reddit
Thread, progress to making a company announcement, and eventually
host an AMA with founders or domain experts. With moderator
support, they may even sticky your AMA announcement at the top of
their subreddit.

Time investment:
ââ Each step requires 2-4 hours per week of consistent engagement

This AMA ranked #1 for âSprout Labs Reviewsâ on Google within 48
hours

------------------------
Why you canât skip steps
------------------------

Everyone wants to jump straight to posting Threads. Theyâre
visible, quantifiable, and easy to report on.

Others try shortcuts: enlisting customers to post for them,
coordinating upvotes, or dropping links to track attribution.
These tactics usually violate Redditâs terms and always damage
long-term reach.

Donât take the bait.

Without a foundation of valuable Comments, your Threads are
fragile. They get removed more easily, attract moderator
scrutiny, and cap your future reach in ways that are hard to
reverse.

Commenting is the unglamorous foundation that makes everything
else work. Itâs how moderators learn to trust you and how you
build a defensible position. Skip it, and youâll be treated
exactly as you are: an outsider trying to extract value rather
than provide it.

-----------------------
How to measure progress
-----------------------

Reddit is a dark-social channel in a link-hostile environment.
That makes measurement harder, but also creates opportunity.

Track two things:

* Platform signals: Karma, impressions, and organic mentions by
other users (we call these âSeeded Mentionsâ)
* Customer signals: Ask leads and purchasers where they heard
about you, and include Reddit as an option

Some brands also track secondary signals like search visibility
and LLM citations. No single metric tells the full story, so
watch leading indicators while compounding effects build in the
background.

------------------------------
Build a system, not a campaign
------------------------------

Successful Reddit programs are built on repeatable processes over
time.

Identify the Threads your brand should join. Map the communityâs
recurring debates. Optimize for Redditâs native signals, not
clicks.

Most of this happens in unowned subredditsâcommunities you donât
control, but where trust and audiences already exist. Creating a
branded subreddit can work, but takes much longer. Most teams
need to prove traction first.

-------------------------
The Reddit organic payoff
-------------------------

Done right, Reddit can become one of the most powerful
compounding assets in a brandâs stack.

You expand your SEO and AEO surface area by participating in (and
later creating) Threads likely to rank in search and get cited by
answer engines. Over time, your brand becomes familiar enough
that others mention it without prompting, compounding the effect.

Engagement Threads extend your reach across the platform. Your
brand name shows up next to genuinely valuable content, providing
a trust halo effect. Eventually, you build an audience that
knows, likes, and trusts you, who are more likely to recommend
and defend you in conversations youâre not even part of.

At that point, youâve built Reddit into a PR megaphone you can
leverage when it matters.

-----------------------------------------
What Reddit rewards (and what it doesnât)
-----------------------------------------

Most social platforms reward you for talking about yourself until
people care. Reddit is the opposite.

On Reddit, the community supplies the context. Your job is to
show up in the right places, add real value, and let that context
associate you with the ideas that matter to your customers.

The brands that win on Reddit are the ones that resist the urge
to promote and focus instead on earning trust through consistent
contribution.

Do that well, and Reddit will give you something most platforms
wonât: influence that compounds over time, reach that doesnât
require paid support, and an audience that recommends you without
prompting.

It takes longer to validate than running ads. Itâs harder to
measure than social channels that allow links. But itâs one of
the only truly compounding organic channels left.

emails.subject: Fragmented Portals to Single Source: AI for LP Operations with Amar Varma of Mantle
emails.body:
View this post on the web at https://tanktalks.substack.com/p/fragmented-portals-to-single-source

In this episode of Tank Talks, Matt Cohen sits down with Amar Varma, CEO and Co-Founder of Mantle, a revolutionary platform designed to transform the private investing landscape. As a serial entrepreneur with experience as both a General Partner (GP) and Limited Partner (LP), Amar offers a rare, dual perspective on the world of investment. His background spans multiple industries, including mobile, connected vehicles, and now private market infrastructure, where he is tackling one of the most persistent pain points: the fragmented, manual world of LP operations.
Amar dives deep into how Mantle is positioned at the intersection of chaos and clarity, automating the heavy lifting to help investors make better decisions without drowning in documents. From global scaling and customer obsession to the parallels between todayâs AI transformation and past tech waves like mobile, this conversation covers a lot of ground. Whether youâre an investor, allocator, or founder, thereâs something for everyone in this episode.
Amar Varmaâs Early Influences and Entrepreneurial Spirit (00:02:15)
Amar shares his journey from growing up in Ottawa to becoming a serial entrepreneur. He talks about his first exposure to tech industries and how a global perspective shaped his career. The experience of being raised in a government and tech hub like Ottawa gave him early access to innovation and a deep curiosity about the world.
The Power of Perseverance and Growth Mindset (00:06:16)
Growing up with an immigrant background, Amar reflects on the importance of perseverance and a growth mindset in overcoming struggles. His belief in the value of individual and team struggles is evident in his journey as a founder, investor, and parent.
The Shift from Founder to Investor (00:13:50)
Amar explains the transition from being a founder to taking a break and exploring the world of investing. His time working as an LP and angel investor gave him insights into the challenges faced by investors, especially when trying to scale operations without sufficient data or structure. This led to his founding of Mantle, which solves many of these problems.
The Birth of Mantle: Revolutionizing LP Operations (00:25:40)
Mantle is designed to automate and streamline the process of managing private market investments. Amar breaks down how Mantleâs software works to track investments, capital calls, K-1s, and investor reports. He discusses the challenges of managing unstructured data and how AI-powered features have allowed Mantle to offer LPs and family offices a more seamless experience.
The Power Law of Venture Capital (00:15:49)
In the world of venture capital, Amar talks about the concept of the power law, how a few investments end up driving the majority of returns. He also discusses the importance of knowing when something is truly working in early-stage investments and how understanding this can lead to better investment decisions.
Family Offices and LP Tech Stacks (00:29:00)
Amar explains how Mantle is helping family offices and LPs with managing their investments, especially when dealing with the unstructured documents that are common in private markets. He shares how Mantle is creating a single source of truth for private assets, helping LPs track their investments across multiple funds, and how AI is helping improve efficiency in this space.
AI-Driven Insights and Workflows (00:32:01)
AI plays a major role in Mantleâs value proposition, helping automate workflows, track financial data, and ensure accuracy across private market investments. Amar dives into the layers of AI that are stitched into Mantleâs platform to help LPs and family offices gain more insight into their portfolios.
The Future of Private Market Investments (00:40:00)
Amar discusses the ongoing evolution of private market investments and the role technology, particularly AI, will play in shaping the future of LP operations. He also reflects on how private market infrastructure is moving towards a more standardized and efficient process, making data more accessible and reliable.
About Amar Varma
Amar Varma is the CEO and Co-Founder of Mantle, a private market infrastructure platform designed to streamline the operations of LPs and family offices. With a background spanning semiconductor design, mobile technology, connected vehicles, and AI, Amar has built multiple successful startups. As an investor and founder, he has gained invaluable insights into the challenges of scaling and managing private market investments.
Connect with Amar Varma on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/amar-varma-8041b9/?originalSubdomain=ca [ https://substack.com/redirect/bc784707-ef6f-498f-a61a-e214fcb70d87?j=eyJ1IjoiMm54eDZsIn0.6_ixIzVSYaJJOOR5VbW4ZiITTg-2zvCjD7bUHJu-17E ]
Visit the Mantle website: https://withmantle.com/ [ https://substack.com/redirect/f364d8a8-9519-4c9c-84ac-20e4b897091c?j=eyJ1IjoiMm54eDZsIn0.6_ixIzVSYaJJOOR5VbW4ZiITTg-2zvCjD7bUHJu-17E ]
Connect with Matt Cohen on LinkedIn: https://ca.linkedin.com/in/matt-cohen1 [ https://substack.com/redirect/372d1b0b-f477-4ffd-b9c3-ea3e4f7348fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMm54eDZsIn0.6_ixIzVSYaJJOOR5VbW4ZiITTg-2zvCjD7bUHJu-17E ]
Visit the Ripple Ventures website: https://www.rippleventures.com/ [ https://substack.com/redirect/13647642-fcef-4f40-8d86-eb508a8b24ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMm54eDZsIn0.6_ixIzVSYaJJOOR5VbW4ZiITTg-2zvCjD7bUHJu-17E ]

Unsubscribe https://substack..rYKzPpfyzK0lxGa5gE8hKqNK43K1SXi0z9PAVN8P-cA?

emails.subject: The Heart in the Machine: Why “Grief-Tech” is the Most Important Frontier
emails.body:
View this post on the web at https://join.nextbig.app/p/the-heart-in-the-machine-why-grief

There is a South Korean documentary called Meeting You that I canât stop thinking about.
In it, a mother puts on a VR headset and a pair of haptic gloves. Sheâs standing in a green-screen studio, but in her eyes, sheâs back in a park.
Suddenly, her seven-year-old daughter, who passed away years prior, runs up to her. The mother reaches out, her hands trembling, trying to touch a digital memory that feels more real than the world around her.
She cries. She talks to her. She gets a moment she thought was stolen forever.
It is one of the most heartbreaking and beautiful uses of technology I have ever seen. And it changes everything about how we view the âbusinessâ of AI.
This Isnât About Immortality. Itâs About the Ache
When we talk about AI and death, we often get caught up in the âcreep factorâ or the sci-fi dream of living forever.
We focus on the tech.
The Microsoft patents for chatbots or the StoryFile AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/0b1336fc-0474-45a7-97d2-b1f39f7ec75f?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] that allowed Marina Smith to speak at her own funeral [ https://substack.com/redirect/3a1ee6f5-e8be-439f-bafd-892533507475?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ].
But if we look closer at that mother in the VR headset, we see the truth: This isnât about the dead. Itâs about the living.
Grief is a heavy, messy, and deeply lonely process. For centuries, weâve relied on faded photos and static memories.
Now, for the first time in human history, we have the tools to make those memories interactive.
We arenât just looking at a photo of a loved one; we are hearing their laugh again, or asking for the advice they never got to give.
Building with Empathy, Not Just Code
As an entrepreneur, I often talk about âdisruptionâ and âmarket gapsâ. But when it comes to Grief-Tech, we have to lead with our hearts. We arenât just building products; we are handling peopleâs most sacred and fragile emotions.
The vital point: the âDigital Pyramidâ.
Just as the pharaohs built monuments to ensure they were never forgotten, we are now building digital legacies. But the real âgoldâ here isnât in creating a perfect AI clone (which, letâs be honest, can be terrifying).
The real opportunity is in softening the blow of loss.
The New Frontier of Human Connection
If youâre looking to build in this space, stop asking âHow can I make an AI live forever?â
Instead, ask: âHow can I help a daughter hear her fatherâs wisdom one last time?â
The Interactive Legacy: Imagine an AI that doesnât âpretendâ to be the deceased, but acts as a guardian of their stories. A digital scrapbook that can answer questions like, âWhat was Momâs favorite song when she was my age?â
Healing Through Presence: Tools like the ones used by that mother in South Korea can provide âclosure sessionsââsafe, controlled environments to say the things left unsaid.
The Ethical Guardian: There is a massive need for platforms that handle âDigital Willsâ with dignity. Who owns your digital heartbeat? Who ensures your AI likeness isnât exploited? This is a space where trust is the only currency that matters.
A Weighted Responsibility
This technology is a double-edged sword. Psychologists worry that staying âconnectedâ to a digital version of a loved one might make it harder to let go. And theyâre right to be concerned.
Thatâs why the next great AI companies in this space wonât just be run by engineers. Theyâll be run by philosophers, grief counselors, and people who understand the sanctity of a human life.
We have the chance to ensure that no oneâs story truly ends when they âlog offâ for the last time.
We can build the digital pyramids that donât just stand tall, but offer a hand to those still walking.
If you could preserve one specific interaction with someone youâve lost, a piece of advice, a joke, a bedtime story, what would it be?
Reply and let me know. Letâs talk about the human side of the machine.
The 2026 Grief-Tech Market Map & Revenue Sheet
The $35.8B market emerging at the intersection of grief and technology. Weâve mapped the 2026 Grief-Tech landscape to show you how âDigital Immortalityâ has moved from a sci-fi dream to a scalable, deeply impactful business model.
Inside the full Premium Sheet, weâre handing you:
The 35+ Power Players: A curated look at the companies turning âfinal goodbyesâ into âdigital presenceâ, from Delphi to Eternos.
The Empathy-Driven Revenue Model: A breakdown of how to price for the most sensitive moment in a customerâs life without losing their trust (SaaS vs. Concierge benchmarks).
The âGrief-Gapâ Analysis: Three specific areas where current tech is failing mourning families and where the next generation of founders will build...

Unsubscribe https://substack..XF56ALOaS27S5lGncbwLSO9X651T0CWJlHuy56aihvk?
emails.snippet: Discover the untapped frontier of Grief-Tech. Learn how to build empathetic AI that preserves legacies and solves the deep human ache of loss. Don't miss out. ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏

The AI boom on Stripe: Lessons from Manus AIâs rapid scale

AI startups are hitting important revenue milestones much faster than previous generations of startups. Manus AI exemplifies this trend: within 8 months of launch, it reached $100 million in ARR for its autonomous AI agent platform.

Join us for an online event with Manus AIâs Cofounder Tao Zhang to hear how the company did itâand for an inside look at what it really takes to build and scale an AI business across global markets.

emails.subject: Elastic Community Newsletter
emails.body:
Elastic Community Newsletter


Hello from the Elastic DevRel team! In this newsletter, we cover version 9.3 of Elasticsearch and the Elastic Stack, the latest blogs and videos, and upcoming events â including the Agent Builder hackathon.


What's New


In version 9.3 of Elasticsearch and the Elastic Stack several features that were introduced in earlier releases are now generally available, including:

- Elastic Agent Builder, for building AI agents that reason over Elasticsearch data
- Pattern-based log compression (pattern_text), reducing log storage costs
- Entity AI Summary in Elastic Security
- Expanded Elastic Inference Service (EIS) model availability, including Jina models <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGFIGX7-f5Q6RuEuf32J_3EzmXOhqiz4E9lSO1SfZPKs0BHh_KTQomD7puJs8fvQUQ-Q=>

9.3 also introduces improvements in vector storage, GPU acceleration, and analytics performance.



Elastic Workflows to build automation within the Elastic Stack

Elastic Workflows <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGTZHSbDAjevFWKvUyOsH6GFEKANnLiL_R9JFqCE5f7lb5XQaigrPMv7R_hbaWzVKmqg=> is an automation engine built into Kibana. You define workflows in YAML; what triggers (starts) them, what steps they take, what actions they perform, and the platform handles execution. A workflow can query Elasticsearch, transform data, branch based on conditions, call external APIs, and integrate with services like Slack, Jira, PagerDuty and more through connectors you've already configured.


A workflow is composed of a few key parts: triggers, inputs, and steps.

Triggers determine when a workflow runs. An alert trigger runs when a Kibana alerting rule fires, with full access to the alert context. A scheduled trigger runs on an interval or cron pattern. A manual trigger runs on demand from the UI or API. A workflow can have multiple triggers.

Inputs define parameters that can be passed to the workflow at runtime. These let you create reusable workflows that accept different values depending on how they're invoked.

Steps are the actions a workflow takes. They execute in sequence, and each step can reference outputs from previous steps. Step types include:

- Internal actions for actions you perform inside Elasticsearch and Kibana, like querying indices, running Elasticsearch Query Language (ES|QL), creating cases, or updating alerts.
- External actions for actions you perform on external systems, like sending a Slack message or creating a Jira ticket. Use any connector you've configured in Elastic, with the flexibility to hit any API or internal service using HTTP steps.
- Flow control for defining the logic of your workflow with conditionals, loops, and parallel execution.
- AI for everything from prompting a large language model (LLM) to enabling agents as workflow steps, unlocking agentic workflow use cases.



bfloat16 support for dense vectors

Elasticsearch 9.3 adds support for storing dense vectors using bfloat16 <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGSEDRCl7Obn5hpV8g6tuSw3ofcqqhQI0Y7Qm5jeiyqMWTrnxb7gFNq3aG-Y6k0wSLUM=> instead of 32-bit floating point values. This reduces vector storage roughly by half and lowers memory pressure, while preserving enough precision for many semantic search and RAG workloads.

This is especially useful for:

- high-dimensional embeddings (e.g. 768, or more dimensions)
- large vector collections
- deployments constrained by memory or disk footprint

Creating an index with bfloat16 vectors:

PUT my-vector-index
{
 "mappings": {
 "properties": {
 "embedding": {
 "type": "dense_vector",
 "dims": 768,
 "element_type": "bfloat16",
 "index": true
 }
 }
 }
}


Indexing documents does not change compared to float-based vectors:

POST my-vector-index/_doc
{
 "embedding": [0.01234567,-0.98123456,0.44319876, "..."]
}


kNN queries use the same syntax as before, with reduced precision handled transparently:

POST my-vector-index/_search
{
 "knn": {
 "field": "embedding",
 "query_vector": [0.02123456,-0.97345678,0.41765432,"..."],
 "k": 10,
 "num_candidates": 30
 }
}


bfloat16 vectors can be combined with disk-based vector indexing and on-disk rescoring to further reduce memory usage for large-scale semantic search workloads.



GPU acceleration for vector indexing and inference

Elastic 9.3 continues to expand GPU support for vector-heavy workloads.

For self-managed deployments, GPU-accelerated vector indexing (technical preview) allows indexing and maintenance tasks to run on NVIDIA GPUs, powered by cuVS. Observed improvements include:

- up to 12x higher vector indexing throughput
- up to 7x faster force-merge operations
- significantly reduced CPU utilization during heavy ingestion

These improvements help reduce time-to-search when building or rebuilding large vector indices.

On the inference side, Elastic Inference Service (EIS) continues to use managed GPU infrastructure for embedding generation and reranking. This allows users to benefit from GPU acceleration without deploying or operating GPUs inside their own Elasticsearch clusters.



ES|QL: faster and more stable time-series analytics
In 9.3, improvements focus on metrics performance and dashboard stability, including:

- Sliding-window aggregations, reducing jitter in time-series visualizations
- Faster execution paths for metrics queries
- Native support for exponential histograms

For example, smoothing a request rate over time can now be expressed directly in ES|QL:

TS metrics
| WHERE TRANGE(1h)
| STATS avg(rate(requests, 10m)) BY TBUCKET(1m), host

These changes make ES|QL better suited for always-on dashboards and operational analytics, not just exploratory queries.


Blogs, Videos, and Interesting Links


Agent Builder: Elastic Agent Builder is now GA. Learn how to quickly develop context-driven AI agents <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGE4EtTUmOaJ3zXdbLikfMroNKiekZ0B92syPRQKiCxEz8vIv7xB55PVfN4qydN7IyH4=> with Anish
Mathur and Evan Castle. Build a voice agent <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGHhZrLY0qCnF-Tf-NV4ZNPp6PsxzrRJ2V8oK7PNngbo_EJrDG0lumvddLpMHUibqFMw=> using Elastic Agent Builder and
LiveKit with Kenneth Kreindler.

Jina AI: Explore Jina models, their functionality,
and how to use them natively in Elasticsearch <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGSDfaShd6PW5ZNWrZ553EInMs60xKeRgW43VT2mPI5wzdY_zk0AVlLTDAb1iP-P8Vx8=> with Scott Martens.

Security: Join Paul Ewing and Sandiya Ramamoorthy as they dive into proactive threat hunting with Elastic Security <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGYgbzvgJT5OzFTdF4YnJBcidn0FgAMP87FR4txRLXRMeQTB6CiBPiMTkdrnhRO2JZ9A=>.

Agentic memory: Learn how to manage agentic
memory in Elasticsearch <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGJzk4YVx5ecykniKxjOxToUt2Sa7FXaNES5KiaZxai_UbHXGlR7Z6uPVlQkPpsvtBAA=> with Someshwaran Mohankumar.

Chunking: Kathleen DeRusso breaks down chunking and
snippet extraction for LLMs <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGD9dEtum_ZnEBUbQmdxipOWEfgitfjSHUrdtw_5RzqzE7__DFudBnB6QKJ2WXUMhoLc=>.

Observability: Frederic Maussion created a practical guide to end-to-end distributed tracing for Nginx with OpenTelemetry in Elastic <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGPsZCZGlt7H4FTgagze4hiVEEw7E1j4Rwqh340QYkCYRZjTAO7HrCweah7glmgSGIeY=>. EDOT Cloud Forwarder for AWS from Elastic Observability is now GA. Learn more about it <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGGJzD6WUOyVGzNN1ayOKT5gLOfvrNXy8sCr4X9bxJMsM19wzRv-9oNcsp3GGqt9JIPQ=> with
Michalis Katsoulis, Andreas Gkizas and Miguel Luna.

Check out these videos:

- How to join Elasticsearch indices using the ES|QL LOOKUP JOIN <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGeWPee0lbpx45bBq_h0-17IN-UbOav7-BzVKoo-jZus18aUpa5uQV6qMcgLVTEzMSl4=> and How to create an Elastic Serverless search project programmatically <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGWojr0MlCqJ-TC1UkL6RfAGzRCzbkVdr1sWRRO_VN0tx8otlx41qwVlmuX1t_MwHino=> by Jeff Vestal

- Parsing logs with AI & Elastic Streams <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGJMTBU5fIWxhbqdX4-Qwk-hnpZ_34RYnXvZRfb0F7pDT7Lxb-XD4eW1ObxrpOp1ie0Q=> by Peter Titov

Featured blogs from the community:

- Building an image-based recommendation system and search engine with deep learning and Elasticsearch <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGHRRorHhqZ5CITb7AMN0bVt1mH5mnl5-Bnybfho7CdLC6gHsPB90TkaS-acaFjzNsIc=> by Carmel Wenga

- Building smart marketplace alerts with Elasticsearch percolate queries <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGVRxF4ifA2JxjM3w5wbz0Xro27kgYE0yJTkN0GZDyg_8HglXRJEgbjLkRQyumxFLEms=> by Abdelkarim Elamel


Upcoming Events


Join the Agent Builder hackathon <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGYGtxMNeTXprbW7CKDdbp0RpAfsZm7ihJBQ24Em7r7IvTPHg7yAM3zCiFMV-XxVz268=> to work with the community to build context-driven, multi-step AI agents. Compete for a share of $20,000 in prizes and get featured on our blog and social channels â deadline February 27.

ElasticOn Tour, the one-day Elastic conference series around the world, is back. Register and join us <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGeZFp-2OAUpb3lsPAWXvIKcbfo6REKbxZdg5Vt9lVurvysSa-kJxKsi0K3CVId8wid4=> in:

- SÃ£o Paulo â March 5
- Sydney â March 5
- Tokyo â March 10
- Singapore â March 17
- Washington, D.C. â March 19

Join your local Elastic User Group chapter for the latest news on upcoming events! You can also find us on Meetup.com <https://link.elastic.co/dc/G9dKwpqRM4h-YQtnndOkOZcAwJsr82JfBPKzf-apomrydfWQQrzFDA8c2HDtN-XV9736V73f0Vl6-8Rx-kUMtpAGvMcXE0xsI_jJPxyXLnbCqKaziQoRAZvcwmxu5gQkxOVLMVC0tBCqa719MZkzmcj5DZoO57GwaqSwBKCYGSrSdk9r4ENTEYZarnfBVS-k/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGFmR6MGovl7wnqDGqo-rLuERJ6SZThXwB-ZQLW5rnQcoP3dN6C11oHBgtzDL5S4q0-M=>.

If youâre interested in presenting at a meetup, send an email to meetups@elastic.co <https://link.elastic.co/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGJtX64lH0juDe9JTxf7pqMITXiFSxzLRjmP1xrCRTL7c4IDWEB7iHx-m1ZxdmJtOaz4=>


Upcoming Events <https://link.elastic.co/dc/G9dKwpqRM4h-YQtnndOkOZcAwJsr82JfBPKzf-apomrydfWQQrzFDA8c2HDtN-XV9736V73f0Vl6-8Rx-kUMtpAGvMcXE0xsI_jJPxyXLnbCqKaziQoRAZvcwmxu5gQkxOVLMVC0tBCqa719MZkzmcj5DZoO57GwaqSwBKCYGSrSdk9r4ENTEYZarnfBVS-k/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGFmR6MGovl7wnqDGqo-rLuERJ6SZThXwB-ZQLW5rnQcoP3dN6C11oHBgtzDL5S4q0-M=>


Developer Resources


Ask questions on Discuss <https://link.elastic.co/dc/jA0KNHBQdxcKzgiBKU9hbiyn8U9WJMkWAh6M1iF-KVwwbal4at21udy1GtvblwxtVOjnzNYNhU8MmqoxqnUBTtuD82rFvhKHlUODlOYGnZ65rakyIjYmew_t5gF4AbBS7RBPPAilzD2sm96SOYMlDWT9JOBnEE0oS6RQoA6zbBSnzyasFitnktxEO5emh_Fn/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGFmR6MGovl7wnqDGqo-rLuERJ6SZThXwB-ZQLW5rnQcoP3dN6C11oHBgtzDL5S4q0-M=>

Visit our YouTube Channel <https://link.elastic.co/dc/M9Y3nowLDCcEv8uSbJAkaUXhEBB5rN7Y_3x87XmxUFQcOWBHihq_VSZ6m6Gbraovsy1pXbQ4I6EoAY3bq8Uko9OA1ZNDia8EjXLqvGJMM-T9oOuux48mUNraW3RqF66TJwNwHIHs3dTWaglt4xqPywvXvRqoMwLxy04HWs0urb_TlgZsJjouOmBRIEWZS2okhVoWbQWQkIo82kck_QsLdplAmocVcvJuxVzfa0xlLoY=/ODEzLU1BTS0zOTIAAAGf8Y6sGFmR6MGovl7wnqDGqo-rLuERJ6SZThXwB-ZQLW5rnQcoP3dN6C11oHBgtzDL5S4q0-M=>

emails.body:
â­ï¸ [Feb 12th] Bond AI SF - Upcoming AI events in SF
Bond AI SF -Â the only independent, curated community featuring in-person events, making it the ultimate dedicated resource for AI events and nothing else. This email is a round-up of curated AI events in SF and the Bay Area and is sent every Thursday morning PT. Want to share your event with the community? Submit it here. Interested in free tickets for top AI conferences such as the upcoming HumanX, AI+ Renaissance Summit, and the Coding Agents Conference? Join our Premium community. â­ï¸ Featured events of the week AI+ Renaissance Summit 2026 with Windsurf, Anthropic, Scale, and more | March 15th A gathering of 40 AI leaders and 20+ top startups exploring frontier advances in agents, multimodal models, AI infra, and robotics. Hear from Parag Agrawal (Parallel, ex-Twitter), Michele Catasta (Replit), Jeff Wang (Windsurf), Emily Xue (Scale), and leaders from DeepMind, Amazon, Composio, and more. With panels, firesides, and live demos, this summit brings together the people and products shaping the next decade of intelligence. Register here (exclusive 20% off for the Bond AI community applied) GenAI, Visibility, Benchmarking & Cost Optimization with AWS + nOps | Feb 19th Join AWS and nOps for a focused session on optimizing GenAI workloads with Amazon Bedrock. Learn how top teams benchmark performance and cut costs. Featuring a fireside chat with AWSâs Kris Skrinak and nOpsâs Jordan Stein, followed by Q&A and networking with cloud leaders. SF AI Engineers with AWS, ThoughtSpot, & Purple AI | March 4th Hear from engineers at Purple AI, ThoughtSpot, and AWS about the hard lessons that come from building and shipping AI systems. Join for real talk and the chance to connect with others building at the application layer of AI. This week Thursday, February 12th â­ï¸ Ski Camp AI: Building Agents and MCP â­ï¸ Fin x Cartesia: Building Voice AI Products â­ï¸ Generative UI Night at WorkOS (Feat A2UI, AG-UI, MCP Apps) OpenClaw Dinner for Engineering Leaders in SF Founder Mixer SPC Demo Night Friday, February 13th â­ï¸ ImagineArt Launch Night Autonomous Robots in Simulation: OM1 + Gazebo Saturday, February 14th AI Meets Robotics â Launch & Fund Your Own Startup Build What You Love - Women in Tech Hackathon Sunday, February 15th OpenClaw / Clawdbot Guided Workshop: Build Your First AI Workflow Live Scrappy AI Founders Go Kayaking Monday, February 16th â­ï¸ Future of AI - Fireside w/ CTO of Microsoft For Startups 90/30 Club (ML reading) #40: Strategist: Self-Improving LLM Decision-Making Tuesday, February 17th â­ï¸ Agentic + AI Observability Meetup SF â­ï¸ Principles of Building Fullstacks Agents at WorkOS (Mastra, CopilotKit & Composio) â­ï¸ Where Startups Win: Data, AI, & Sport Innovation Skills Launch Party Wednesday, February 18th â­ï¸ Voices of Voice AI â­ï¸ AI Night ~ w/ DigitalOcean, Founder's Garage & Jam.dev Get Grilled by YC Backed Founders - Vibe Coding Hackathon German founder mixer - Zero Prime & Speedinvest Upcoming events Thursday, February 19th â­ï¸ GenAI: Tech Deep Dive into Real-Time Video Generation Identity as the Attack Surface: Architecting Auth for Autonomous Agents Friday, February 20th â­ï¸ AWS x Datadog SF GenAI Hackathon â $30K+ in Prizes Saturday, February 21st AI for Good: Future of Education Open Source Build Scrappy AI Founders Go Dirt-Biking Monday, February 23rd â­ï¸ AI Dev Tool Demo Night @ AWS Builder Loft (#4th Edition) NEARCON 2026 SF Tuesday, February 24th â­ï¸ MCP Compositional Architecture Workshop The AI Hustle: Happy Hour for Startup Founders & VCs Hire or Get Hired - AI Matching Your Next Role Wednesday, February 25th â­ï¸ Engineering & AI After Hours â­ï¸ She Ships: AI Lightning Demo Night Thursday, February 26th â­ï¸ Operators Happy Hour by Chore: The Rise of AI Agents â Claude, Automation Friday, February 27th Autonomous Agents Hackathon Saturday, February 28th AI Youth Hackathon with Lovable Scrappy AI Founders Play Pickleball Tuesday, March 3rd â­ï¸ Coding Agents: AI Driven Dev Conference with Anthropic, Graphite, LangChain, Warp, OpenHands, and more Apply our exclusive community code for 20% off BONDAI_CODE Wednesday, March 4th â­ï¸ SF AI Engineers w/ AWS, ThoughtSpot, & Purple AI Autonomous Cloud Commitment Management From Idea to Impact: Women in AI Panel & Pitch Night Thursday, March 5th â­ï¸ SF AGI Royale Gala with AI operators from FAANG, Stanford, Berkeley, and more Scaling AI the Cloud Native Way Sunday, March 8th â­ï¸ Daytona Compute feat. LangChain, Sentry, LiveKit, Amp, Factory, and more Apply our exclusive community code for 30% off BONDAI_DAYTONA Thursday, March 12th UiPath DevConnect 2026 - SF Workshop Sunday, March 15th â­ï¸ AI+ Renaissance Summit 2026 with Windsurf, Anthropic, Scale, and more Apply our exclusive community code for 20% off GENSF Monday, April 6th â­ HumanX - World's premier AI conference feat. CEOs from Sierra, Amazon, Databricks, ElevenLabs, Slack, Cohere, and more Apply our exclusive community code for $850 off HX26P_AITIDBITS ð More AI events on our Luma page. Want to share your event with the community? Submit it here. Want your friends/colleagues to also know about the best AI events? Tell them to subscribe here. Based elsewhere? Hi Moe,

Just checking in to see if you would be available for a brief introduction call in the coming weeks.

Let me know when would work best for you and I will send over a calendar invite.

Best,
Beri

Beri Lantum | Silversmith Capital Partners | o: 617-571-0948





On Mon, Feb 9, 2026 at 9:04 am, Beri Lantum wrote:

Hey Moe,

I understand you are busy and may not be interested in raising capital at this time, but I would still love to chat and learn more about Pica. Let me know what day/time would work best for a quick call.

Best,
Beri

Beri Lantum | Silversmith Capital Partners | o: 617-571-0948

Just a quick bump on this -- thanks!

Best,
Beri

On Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 5:15 pm, Beri Lantum wrote:

Hi Moe,

Hope you're doing well.

I have been following Pica for some time and wanted to introduce myself and Silversmith, a $5B growth equity firm based in Boston. My team and I have been spending time in AI agent integrations, and I am impressed with how your platform gives agents reliable, secure connections to 200+ SaaS tools so they can actually take real actions.

We invest in fast-growing, founder-led companies within the tech & healthcare sectors, with investments including Webflow, Gearset, Apryse, Microblink, eOne Solutions, Appfire, Proven Optics, and other developer tool and IT-focused businesses.

If you have 15 minutes, I would love to introduce Silversmith and learn more about Pica. What is the best way to get on your calendar for a brief call in the next few weeks?

Thank you in advance and looking forward to speaking.

Best,
Beri

Beri Lantum
Silversmith Capital Partners

116 Huntington Ave., 15th Floor
Boston, MA 02116

emails.subject: Thank you for signing up to Passionfroot!
emails.body:
Hey Moe,

Welcome to Passionfroot! You have been approved to the platform and I'm
excited to kick off your 14 day free trial period. Here
<https://share.descript.com/view/mg6LTr4eGqD>'s a short demo to help you
get started. You can also jump straight into the product - I've outlined
suggested next steps below.

*Suggested Next Steps*

   1. *Shortlist the creators you want to work with* using our AI agent Zest
   <https://workspace.passionfroot.me/partners/zest>. It will help you
   shape your campaign based on your ICP, your goals, and insights from what
   has worked for similar brands.
   2. *Reach out to the selected creators*. Zest
   <https://workspace.passionfroot.me/partners/zest> will assist here as
   well by sending a tailored message to each creator.
   3. Top up your wallet <https://workspace.passionfroot.me/partners/wallet>.
   This is the easiest way to pay creators - add funds once and pay out
   instantly when campaigns go live.

*What Happens Next*
Once creators respond, review and approve their proposals. You can pay them
directly using the Passionfroot payment link they share.
