emails.subject: The AI fight gets political
emails.body:
----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ba05790b-b8f9-4f69-a529-98b84cf7c990/Subscribers_only_with_lock__transparent_.png?t=1765488456)
Caption: 


----------

**Welcome back.**

The AI safety debate has officially graduated from boardroom philosophy to bare-knuckle electoral politics â Anthropic is pouring $20 million into a pro-regulation PAC, setting up a direct spending war against the $125 million war chest assembled by OpenAI executives and Andreessen Horowitz ahead of November's midterms. 

The clash is exposing a widening GOP schism on tech oversight and raising an uncomfortable question for AI companies: can you credibly fund the rules for a race you're also trying to win?

Letâs dive in.

 

----------
ð¡_  You are reading a free preview of the full edition usually reserved for __[Daily Pass](https://thatstartupguy.beehiiv.com/upgrade)__ members_


----------_ _ 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8df0059e-7a37-4235-ac23-84ed48359bc8/driving_the_day.png?t=1764072838)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/31c32197-6603-42f4-87f6-3948d95a4a51/adam.jpg?t=1770902928)
Follow image link: (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/11/technology/adam-mosseri-instagram-addiction-trial.html)
Caption: 

[**â**](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/11/technology/adam-mosseri-instagram-addiction-trial.html)** Instagram Chief Tells Court Social Media Isn't "Clinically Addictive"**
Adam Mosseri took the stand in a landmark tech addiction trial, comparing social media habits to binge-watching a good TV show â but internal documents revealed he and Zuckerberg overruled colleagues to reinstate plastic surgeryâstyle beauty filters despite warnings they'd cause body dysmorphia in teens, a move one Meta exec warned would "rightly" look like putting growth over responsibility
 
[**â**](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/11/bill-ackman-meta-valuation.html)** Bill Ackman Takes a Big Bite of Meta, Calls It "Deeply Discounted"** 
Pershing Square disclosed a new Meta position worth 10% of fund capital, arguing that Wall Street is punishing the stock â down 16% over the past year â for AI capex that could reach $135 billion in 2026 while ignoring the long-term earnings upside those bets are designed to unlock
 
[**â**](https://www.theinformation.com/briefings/x-subscriptions-hit-1-billion-annualized-revenue-exec-says)** X Hits $1 Billion in Annualized Subscription Revenue** 
Elon Musk's social platform has quietly crossed the ten-figure mark in recurring subscription income â spanning $3-to-$40-a-month tiers that bundle checkmarks, boosted posts, and access to xAI's Grok models â a milestone disclosed during an xAI all-hands that also revealed a broader leadership shakeup following the company's merger with SpaceX


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4fdf63c9-ad3a-4960-b2e3-2cb30f5c14aa/strategy_blue_anim_one_loop.gif?t=1753437621)
Caption: 

###### News Analysis | **PAC to the future**

### _**Anthropic drops $20M to fight OpenAI, a16z lobbying effort**_

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3ddea3c3-6789-44cb-8a96-b9d444e13942/claude.jpg?t=1770902288)
Follow image link: (https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/anthropic-enters-midterm-election-showdown-over-ai-regulation-1541556e?mod=hp_lead_pos6)
Caption: 


--------------------
**Whatâs Happening**
The AI industry's policy debate has spilled into electoral politics â and the checks being written have a lot of zeroes. Anthropic is donating $20 million to Public First, a group pushing for AI guardrails and chip-export restrictions, directly squaring off against Leading the Future, a $125 million-plus super-PAC network backed by OpenAI executives and Andreessen Horowitz. 

The clash promises to make AI regulation a defining issue in November's midterms, with both sides now bankrolling candidates and ad campaigns. What was once a boardroom disagreement over safety philosophy is now a full-blown Washington spending war.

* **The money race is escalating fast**
Anthropic's $20 million infusion brings Public First's initial fundraising target to $75 million, up from $50 million â though it still trails Leading the Future's $125 million war chest. Public First, organized as a 501(c)(4), isn't required to disclose its donors, leaving room for other deep-pocketed contributors to quietly pile in

  * The group is already running ads backing Sen. Marsha Blackburn's gubernatorial bid in Tennessee and supporting Sen. Pete Ricketts in Nebraska, both Republicans who favor stricter AI oversight and chip-export controls

* **A GOP schism is widening**
President Trump signed an executive order targeting state-level AI regulation, but figures like Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis have publicly opposed it, favoring the kind of guardrails Anthropic champions. That intra-party divide hands groups like Public First an unusual opening: the ability to back Republican candidates on a traditionally Democrat-coded issue

  * Meanwhile, the Defense Department is reportedly weighing whether to cancel an Anthropic contract over the company's restrictions on military AI use â a reminder that Anthropic's safety-first positioning carries real commercial risk

* **Competitors cry foul**
Industry leaders have accused Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei of wielding safety regulation as a competitive weapon, arguing that stricter rules would disproportionately burden rivals. Anthropic calls those claims baseless, but the optics are complicated: the company is simultaneously building frontier AI models and funding the political infrastructure to regulate them

  * The tension between "we build it" and "we want to constrain it" will likely define how voters and policymakers judge the sincerity of the safety argument

**Why It Matters**:
AI regulation has graduated from white papers and Senate hearings to hard-dollar electoral combat, and the midterms will serve as the first real referendum on where Americans â or at least their representatives â want the guardrails drawn. The dueling PAC networks also signal a deeper fracture within the AI industry itself: one camp views regulation as an existential brake on innovation, the other as an existential necessity. 

With states proposing a patchwork of AI bills and no federal framework in sight, the outcome of these races could determine whether the U.S. gets coherent national policy or a maze of conflicting state rules. Add chip-export restrictions and defense contracts to the mix, and the stakes extend well beyond Silicon Valley into national security and geopolitics. The AI lobby war is just getting started.

**[Go Deeper ](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/anthropic-enters-midterm-election-showdown-over-ai-regulation-1541556e?mod=hp_lead_pos6)****[ ](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/anthropic-enters-midterm-election-showdown-over-ai-regulation-1541556e?mod=hp_lead_pos6)****[â](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/anthropic-enters-midterm-election-showdown-over-ai-regulation-1541556e?mod=hp_lead_pos6)**


--------------------
âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ


---------- 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/da8f1fca-3784-4896-9fd9-821309b33fc1/Copy_of_VC_Corner__1_.png?t=1768568013)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4b73cf1a-95d7-491d-a809-72716492c238/siri.jpg?t=1770902133)
Follow image link: (https://www.wired.com/story/ai-industry-rivals-are-teaming-up-on-a-startup-accelerator/)
Caption: 

**[â](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-11/apple-s-ios-26-4-siri-update-runs-into-snags-in-internal-testing-ios-26-5-27)** **Appleâs Latest Attempt to Launch New Siri Runs Into Snags**
Appleâs long-promised Siri overhaulâmeant to let the assistant tap personal data, control apps fluidly and rival chatbot-style competitorsâhas stumbled in internal testing with latency, accuracy and architectural bugs, pushing key features from iOS 26.4 toward later releases and underscoring how hard it is for a privacy-first incumbent to retrofit generative AI into a billion-device ecosystem
 
**[â](https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/anthropic-cover-costs-electricity-price-increases-data-centers-rcna258554)** **Anthropic to cover costs of electricity price increases from its data centers**
As it embarks on a multibillion-dollar data-center build-out, Anthropic says it will fund grid upgrades, invest in new generation and reimburse utilities for consumer price hikesâan attempt to pre-empt the political backlash that has followed soaring power bills in AI-heavy regions and to argue that infrastructure, not households, should bear the cost of the AI arms race
 
**[â](https://www.wsj.com/finance/stocks/big-tech-accounting-creates-a-blind-spot-in-the-ai-boom-e2d7d1eb?mod=tech_feat1_ai_pos1)** **Big Tech Accounting Creates a Blind Spot in the AI Boom**
With Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft and Oracle set to pour trillions into AI infrastructure, soaring depreciation expenses are increasingly buried across income-statement line items, leaving investors to model margins through a fog just as the sector shifts from asset-light code to asset-heavy concrete and chipsâa transparency gap regulators wonât fully close until 2028 


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4fdf63c9-ad3a-4960-b2e3-2cb30f5c14aa/strategy_blue_anim_one_loop.gif?t=1753437621)
Caption: 

###### News Analysis | **The cost of intelligence**

### _**OpenAI still has a cash problem**_

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/331dd427-5689-4e4f-9ea2-c8086bd80f35/openai.jpg?t=1770901721)
Follow image link: (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/11/technology/openai-revenue-challenge.html)
Caption: 


--------------------
**Whatâs Happening**
OpenAI finds itself in an uncomfortable position: spending like a tech giant while earning like a startup. The company pulled in roughly $13 billion last year but expects to burn through $100 billion over the next four years on computing infrastructure â a gap that no amount of ChatGPT subscriptions can close. 

To bridge it, OpenAI is simultaneously launching an ads business inside ChatGPT, pushing aggressively into enterprise software, and floating novel "value sharing" arrangements where it would take a cut of discoveries made using its tools. The irony is thick: Sam Altman once called ads "a last resort." That time has arrived.

* **The ads gamble that could backfire**
OpenAI has begun serving advertisements inside ChatGPT's free tier, targeting the 94% of its 800 million users who don't pay for subscriptions. The company has hired hundreds of employees from Meta and X to build its ad infrastructure, but industry veterans compare the effort to Netflix's two-year slog to build a viable ads business â time OpenAI may not have

  * The deeper risk is reputational: Anthropic already ran a Super Bowl ad mocking the move, and users who chose ChatGPT partly for its clean interface may reconsider if answers start arriving with commercial baggage

* **Enterprise is the real battleground**
OpenAI wants business revenue to reach 50% of the total by year's end, up from roughly 40% today, through products like Codex and ChatGPT Enterprise. But the enterprise AI market is crowded and getting more competitive by the quarter â Google and Microsoft have decades of sales relationships, while Anthropic's Claude Code is gaining traction in the lucrative AI-assisted coding segment

  * UBS analyst Karl Keirstead notes that average businesses may balk at paying up to $200 per seat for tools that increasingly have capable alternatives

* **"Value sharing" spooked the wrong crowd**
CFO Sarah Friar's Davos comments about taking a cut of scientific discoveries made with OpenAI tools triggered a swift backlash from researchers â exactly the power users the company needs as evangelists. OpenAI scrambled to clarify that individual scientists wouldn't be affected, but the damage to trust was done

  * The episode illustrates a recurring pattern: OpenAI floating aggressive monetization ideas before thinking through how they'll land with the people who actually use the products

**Why It Matters**:
OpenAI's revenue challenge is really a clock problem. The company needs to triple revenue to roughly $39 billion this year while simultaneously building an ads business from scratch, competing for enterprise contracts against entrenched incumbents, and convincing Wall Street it's IPO-ready â possibly by December. Each of these would be a formidable undertaking on its own; attempting all four simultaneously risks doing none of them well. 

The broader implication for the AI industry is sobering: if the most well-funded startup in history struggles to turn transformative technology into a sustainable business, it raises hard questions about the economics underpinning the entire sector's valuations. For competitors like Anthropic and Google, OpenAI's scramble represents both an opening and a cautionary tale about the gap between building impressive AI and building a durable company around it.

**[Go Deeper](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/11/technology/openai-revenue-challenge.html)****[ ](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/11/technology/openai-revenue-challenge.html)****[â](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/11/technology/openai-revenue-challenge.html)**




---------- 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6f23f859-2a04-469c-a5dd-5e74e43df7b7/VC_Corner_pyramids.png?t=1743020798)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f9be833-e525-49e6-8580-83f7b98e0a2c/both.jpg?t=1770901430)
Follow image link: (https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-11/vcs-rush-to-back-rivals-openai-and-anthropic-as-ai-frenzy-mounts)
Caption: 




--------------------
#### **â**** ****Deal of the Day**

Anthropic is nearing the close of a $20 billion round co-led by **Founders Fund, D.E. Shaw, Dragoneer, Iconiq, **and** MGX** at a roughly $350 billion valuation. That investor roster tells you everything about where the capital is coming from: a quant hedge fund, a sovereign-adjacent vehicle, a Tiger cub descendant, none of which are traditional venture leads (aside from Peter Thielâs Founders Fund). 

Two of the largest private fundraises in history (OpenAI and Anthropic) are drawing from the same investor pool because there is not enough institutional capital willing to underwrite frontier AI risk at this scale, and firms like Sequoia, Altimeter, and Blackstone are now backing both OpenAI and Anthropic rather than picking a winner. 

The old norm of serial monogamy in venture depended on founders having leverage over their cap tables. When a company needs $20 billion in a single raise, that leverage inverts, and the largest pools of capital set the terms.

**What to Watch Next**
Whether dual-backing at the foundation layer normalizes the same behavior downstream, with growth funds quietly taking positions in competing vertical AI startups in legal, healthcare, and coding where the conflict-of-interest calculus is far harder to dismiss.


----------âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/dabefb31-3760-4139-888d-07eca01f1967/Copy_of_VC_Corner__2_.png?t=1768568090)
Caption: 

----------
**[â](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-11/apptronik-raises-520-million-in-new-funding-to-build-more-humanoids?srnd=phx-technology)** **Apptronik Raises $520M **â Now valued at over $5.5B, the Austin humanoid robotics startup is pushing Apollo from pilot programs into scaled production, betting that general-purpose robots can finally work alongside humans in factories and beyond
 
**[â](https://www.fiercehealthcare.com/health-tech/care-navigation-startup-garner-health-scores-118m-series-d-135b-valuation)** **Garner Health Raises $118M Series D **â With a dataset spanning 60B+ medical records, the care navigation startup ranks doctors across 700 performance metrics and uses employer-funded incentives to nudge patients toward higher-quality, lower-cost care
 
**[â](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/11/twilio-co-founders-fusion-power-startup-raises-450m-from-bessemer-and-alphabets-gv/)** **Inertia Enterprises Raises $450M Series A **â Co-founded by Twilioâs Jeff Lawson, the fusion venture aims to commercialize inertial confinement energy by building ultra-high-frequency laser systems capable of powering grid-scale plants in the next decade
 
**[â](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/11/ai-inference-startup-modal-labs-in-talks-to-raise-at-2-5b-valuation-sources-say/)** **Meridian Raises $17M Seed **â Targeting bankers and finance teams, the New York startup is rethinking spreadsheets as an AI-native, auditable workspace designed to reduce hallucinations and compress hours of modeling into minutes
 
**[â](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/11/meridian-ai-raises-17-million-to-remake-the-agentic-spreadsheet/)** **Monaco Raises $35M **â Former Founders Fund VC Sam Blondâs new sales tech startup pairs AI agents with experienced human operators, positioning itself as an AI-native CRM alternative for early-stage companies
 
**[â](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/11/former-founders-fund-vc-sam-blond-launches-ai-sales-startup-to-upend-salesforce/)** **Modal Labs in Talks to Raise at $2.5B Valuation **â The inference infrastructure company is reportedly negotiating a new round that would underscore investor appetite for platforms that help developers reliably run and scale large AI models in production


----------âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ba45286f-4769-46ee-9006-0d5973d48a3d/one_more._thing.png?t=1764072872)
Caption:

âââââââââââââââââââââââââââ

emails.subject: Team in SEA
emails.body:
Moe, seems already has dev teams in SEA, interesting.

If you ever need part of your team fully synced with US working hours, LatAm tends to integrate very naturally into distributed setups like yours.

I could list why our talent is different, but easier to just show you:

FlipSystem tried multiple vendors but couldn't find a team that felt like their own. We built a 6-person squad across dev, QA, and design that clicked from day one. What started as staffing turned into a long-term partnership: same team, full ownership, still shipping together.

Is this on your 2026 roadmap?

Best regards,
Leandro Boffi
CEO at Southteams

PS: This is just one example. We've worked with 20+ clients across every major stack. Happy to send referrals if needed.

emails.subject: Who's who in crypto IPOs
emails.body:
Apptronik rakes in $520M for humanoid robotics; two-tier VC market blooms in Europe; fusion energy startup Inertia scores $450M Series A
February 12, 2026   |   Read online   |   Manage your subscription
The Daily Pitch
VC
Presented by SVB
 
ð Strong job growth in healthcare helped produce the best month for job gains in more than a year, the Labor Department says. What's ahead? In our latest Healthcare Services Report, PitchBook analysts see signs of a 2026 rebound after a fourth-quarter lull. Get it here

ðï¸ Sign up for our Capital Pool newsletter to get weekly commentary on what's driving LP decision-making.
 
VCs have high hopes for 2026 crypto IPOs
(PitchBook News)
By Rachel Schlueter and Michael Bodley, PitchBook News

Even with the past year's crop of cryptocurrency IPOs having suffered massive losses amid bitcoin's plunge, a new batch of crypto companies is expected to go public in 2026.

Potential contenders are already lining up. Kraken, a crypto exchange specialist, filed confidentially for an IPO in the fourth quarter. Consensys, the parent company of the popular wallet MetaMask, and Ledger, a hardware wallet manufacturer, are reportedly beginning to prep listings.

Many of the current investors in these still-private companies took their stakes when the firms were nascent upstarts and at much lower valuations. They've now been waiting a long time to cash outâin some cases, over a decade.

With the Trump administration's pro-crypto stance and Wall Street's growing appetite for stablecoins and other digital asset infrastructure, the industry is seizing the moment.

But public market investors are finding out the hard way that the shorter-term prospects of these stocks are closely tied to the ups and downs of cryptocurrency prices.

"It really just comes down to most cryptocurrency companies are very heavily exposed to both the price of cryptocurrency and then the amount of interest there is in cryptocurrency, which tends to be influenced by price performance," said Michael Miller, a Morningstar analyst who covers Coinbase.

But one potential haven could be companies with strong stablecoin businesses, such as Kraken. Stablecoins don't have the volatility of traditional cryptocurrencies because their value is linked to the underlying investments, generally US treasuries.

"One of the big challenges for cryptocurrency companies in general is to try to find more stable recurring revenue sources," Miller said. "So that's why you've seen stablecoins be a source for that."
Go deeper
 
Related story: Crypto companies finally cracked the IPO ceiling in 2025
 
A message from SVB  
SVB releases 2026 State of the Markets
 
SVBâs new report finds venture gaining steam â $340B invested and exits are up â while fundraising tightens and round timelines stretch.

Get the full report
 
Catch Up Quick  
â¢ Humanoid robotics startup Apptronik notched $520 million, benefiting from an explosion of VC dealmaking fueled by AI enthusiasm in the space. Keep reading

â¢ Blue Owl landed $3 billion for its first secondaries strategy, which co-CEO Marc Lipschultz says can bring "the greatest hits of private equity" to institutional and wealthy individual investors. Find out more

â¢ Nuveen has agreed to take London-listed Schroders private at a Â£9.9 billion (about $13.3 billion) valuation, a deal that would create a $2.5 trillion investment giant. Read the latest
 
Perceptions can trump reality in the continuation fund market
By Jessica Hamlin, Senior Funds Columnist

When pressed on what could derail the continuation-fund market, people often say it's the perception that managers are holding onto companies not because they want to, but because nobody wants to buy them.

On the surface, Frazier Healthcare Partners' continuation fund for Accuity Delivery Systems appears to be such a case. San Francisco buyout shop Frazier planned to sell Accuity Delivery Systems to UnitedHealthcare for roughly $1.1 billion, but the insurer pulled out in the final hour, PitchBook reported.

Unable to secure another satisfactory bid, Frazier opted to hold onto its stake in the healthcare document management business with the financial support of Barings, Partners Group and CPP Investments.

Accuity is a strong performer for Frazier, according to sources familiar with the company. The business is marked by the GP at a 3.8x multiple, and people with knowledge of the company believe it will eventually sell for the right price. "There are so many bad continuation funds out thereâthis isn't one," said one LP.

The involved parties would surely benefit from being open about the rationale behind the deal.
 
Continuation funds, used by general partners to hold onto companies while meeting their investors' liquidity demands, drove the secondary market to record heights in 2025.
Read our original article: Frazier Healthcare shops CV after UnitedHealthcare deal falls through
 
Side Letters  
Smart reads that caught our eye.

â¢ Elizabeth Warren continues to push against PE in healthcare. The senator reintroduced a bill to crack down on PE practices in the healthcare industry that she and her co-sponsors consider exploitative. [Bloomberg Law]

â¢ Rich Ruohonen is a personal injury attorney from Minnesota, and could also break a Winter Olympic record. The 54-year-old curler could become the oldest athlete to represent the US in Winter Olympics history. [The Wall Street Journal]

â¢ A former Google executive says some advanced degrees will no longer be worth it in the age of AI. Higher education as we know it is on the verge of becoming obsolete, Jad Tarifi told Fortune. [Fortune]

VC Deals  
Fusion energy startup Inertia Enterprises raised a $450 million Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners.

London-based AI chip startup Olix raised $220 million in a round led by Hummingbird Ventures.

Loyal, which is developing a drug to extend the lifespan of dogs, secured a $100 million Series C led by Age1.

Berlin-based Andercore, an industrial trade platform provider, secured a $40 million Series B from investors including Atomico, Project A and Inven Capital.

Cancer care company Atlas Oncology Partners raised a $28 million Series A led by Flare Capital Partners.

The Biological Computing Co., a startup using neural systems to build AI infrastructure, raised a $25 million seed round led by Primary.

Enterprise cybersecurity startup Nucleus Security secured a $20 million Series C led by Delta-v Capital.

Munich-based mining startup Hades received a â¬15 million seed investment led by HV Capital and Headline.
 
PE Deals  
Pimco is backing a Â£3 billion debt refinancing deal by CVC Capital Partners' Global Sport Group, Sky News reported.

PE firms are considering an acquisition of Waygate Technologies, the industrial inspection equipment unit of Baker Hughes, which could be valued at around $1.5 billion in a deal, Bloomberg reported.

Armira Growth made a â¬30 million strategic investment in Aviloo, an Austrian EV battery diagnostic company.

LDC agreed to invest in Eagle Eye Innovations, a UK-based drone training and operational support service.

Mauritius-based Phatisa agreed to acquire Zaad Group, a South African seed research company.
 
Exits  
SoftBank-backed Icertis, which makes software for managing business contracts, is exploring a sale that could value the company at as much as $5 billion, Bloomberg reported.

Court Square Capital Partners agreed to sell construction products company Kodiak Building Partners to NYSE-listed QXO in a $2.25 billion deal.

Singapore's sovereign wealth fund GIC is considering a sale of its majority stake in a European-based data center joint venture, which could be valued at around â¬2 billion in a deal, Bloomberg reported.

American Securities-backed Solv Energy, which provides services in the energy sector, raised $512.5 million in its IPO.

Sanabil Investments-backed Richard Attias & Associates, the organizer of Saudi Arabia's Future Investment Initiative summit, is working with Citigroup and SNB Capital for an IPO, Bloomberg reported.
 
Fundraising  
Vision Ridge Partners, a real assets-focused investor, closed its fourth Sustainable Asset Fund on $2.4 billion.

Montana Capital Partners closed its MCP Opportunity Secondary Program VI fund on $1.4 billion.

Eurazeo held a first close of its latest sustainable maritime infrastructure fund on â¬175 million.

Paris-based Elaia raised â¬120 million in the first close of its fifth digital venture fund.

Phatisa held an $86 million first close for its third food fund.
 
Investors  
Paris-based startup campus Station F launched an accelerator program for AI startups.

emails.subject: [Action Advised] Review Google Cloud credential security best practices
emails.body:
Secure service account and API keys to prevent unauthorized access.




MY CONSOLE




Hello Moe,

We're writing to provide you with security best practices regarding the  
management of service account keys and API keys within your Google Cloud  
environment.

Recent security trends indicate that long-lived credentials without proper  
security best practices remain a top security risk for unauthorized access.  
To ensure your environment remains secure, and to modernize your  
authentication strategy, we strongly advise implementing the unified  
security framework outlined below.

What you need to do

Action advised:

Secure the credential lifecycle: Apply standard security hygiene by  
following these best practices:


Zero-Code Storage: Never commit keys to source code or version control. Use  
Secret Manager to inject credentials at runtime.
Disable Dormant Keys: Audit your active keys and decommission any that show  
no activity over the last 30 days.
Enforce API Restrictions: Never leave an API key unrestricted. Limit keys  
to specific APIs (eg, Maps Java Script only) and apply environmental  
restrictions (IP addresses, HTTP referrers, or bundle IDs).
Apply Least Privilege: Never give full permissions to a service account.  
Use the IAM recommender to prune unused permissions for service accounts,  
ensuring only the absolute minimum access required for their function.
Mandatory Rotation: Implement the iam.serviceAccountKeyExpiryHours policy  
to enforce a maximum lifespan for all user-managed service account keys. If  
service account keys are not needed, implement  
iam.managed.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation to disable the creation of new  
service account keys.

Improve operational safeguards: Ensure a rapid response to security  
incidents by completing the following:


Set Essential Contacts: Verify that your Essential Contacts are up to date  
to ensure critical security notifications reach the right people during an  
incident.
Set Billing Anomaly and Budget Alerts: Ensure billing anomaly and budget  
alerts notifications are acted on. A sudden spike in consumption is often  
the first indicator of a compromised credential.

We're here to help

We are committed to helping you maintain a secure environment. If you have  
any questions or require assistance, please contact Google Cloud Support.

Thanks for choosing Google Cloud.





â The Google Cloud Team





DOCUMENTATION



SUPPORT




Was this information helpful?

Yes Neutral No


Â© 2026 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You've received this mandatory service announcement to update you about  
important changes to Google Cloud or your account.



Visit Google Cloud blog Visit GCP on GitHub Visit Google Cloud on LinkedIn  
Visit Google Cloud on Twitter

emails.subject: Your RBC Royal Bank eStatement is ready
emails.body:
BUILDABLE TECHNOLOGIES INC. ,

The following RBC Royal Bank eStatement(s) is(are) available:

Business Deposit Account XXXXX-XXX7169
Business Deposit Account XXXXX-XXX5302

To see your statement, please sign in to RBC Online Banking^.

To review or change your email notification options within RBC Online Banking, select "Profile and Preferences" from the Banking tab under My Accounts.

Regular, unencrypted email is not secure. Never include personal or confidential information in a regular email. To discuss your personal information with us safely, send us a message via the RBC Royal Bank Online Banking message centre or one of our other secure message centres, or contact us by phone.

RBC will never send a regular email that asks you to provide, confirm or verify personal, login or account information. Also, RBC will never include a link to an online service in a regular email and ask you to sign in using that link. If you receive an email of this type, that appears to be from RBC, please forward it to phishing@rbc.com and then delete it. For more information please visit Email & Website Fraud.

If you need any help, please call us:

emails.subject: Beta Update (Feb 11) Build an OpenClaw-Style AI Teammate (No Code, $1K Prize)
emails.body:
View this post on the web at https://betauniversity.substack.com/p/beta-update-feb-11-build-an-openclaw

Welcome to this weekâs Beta Update ð 
*Note: Youâre receiving this update because you previously registered for a Beta Universityâhosted or co-hosted event via our Luma calendar [ https://substack.com/redirect/f2c093da-edfb-4a00-bf9d-0389fa747a4a?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] and opted in to receive communications from the hosts.
ð Coming up
Thursday 2/12: Turn AI Into a Teammate Without Writing Code - $1,000 in Cash Prizes ð [ https://substack.com/redirect/a7c8be14-06ed-40f5-acdc-4dd196e372b0?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ]
For non-technical builders
P.S.: âââIf you want to help support the next event, drop us a note at gladys@betauniversity.org for sponsorship inquiries.
ðTurn AI Into a Teammate
The reality of the current AI moment is simple: Most people are playing with toys, while only a handful are building tools. Weâve all spent hours experimenting with platforms like Clawbot, only to realize that while the output is impressive, it hasnât actually integrated into our daily workflow. We are stuck in the âchatâ phase, treating agents like sophisticated search engines rather than the automated teammates they were designed to be.
Tomorrow, we move past the chat box.
In collaboration with MuleRun, we are opening the doors to an intimate, hands-on workshop in Palo Alto for those ready to graduate from AI spectators to AI architects. Weâre bypassing high-level forecasts in favor of a clinical, step-by-step masterclass. To raise the stakes, we are putting our money where our mouth is with a total of $1,000 in prizes for the most impactful workflows. We will award $100 each to the top three Agents built live during the session, judged on practical execution and clarity. The remaining $700 will be awarded post-event to the architects who successfully scale their impact through our referral leaderboardâincluding a $300 grand prize for the top-performing agent. We want to see who can bridge the gap between 'interesting' and 'indispensable' most effectively under pressure.
We are stripping away the âAI noiseâ to focus on the architecture of a practical teammate. Youâll bring a real-world workflowâthe one that currently drains your cognitive energyâ, and we will spend the afternoon deconstructing it, step by step, until it is ready for automation. No code, no fluff, just the raw logic of âClawbot-styleâ thinking applied to your specific career.
The room is capped at 50. We are down to the final few invitations. If you are ready to stop prompting and start architecting, weâll see you in Palo Alto.
Secure one of the final seats here [ https://substack.com/redirect/a7c8be14-06ed-40f5-acdc-4dd196e372b0?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ]
ð¥ Calling all founders
Apply Beta 8-Week Pre-Acceleration Program
Witnessing the energy at our events is just the beginning. The Beta University Pre-Acceleration Program [ https://substack.com/redirect/c18e034c-f935-41c4-a8d4-a46bdf69a5cd?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] is your free, 8-week ticket to building a VC-fundable business with Silicon Valleyâs proven playbook.
This is where you start building your own story and join the ranks of our celebrated alumni, including: Adsgency AI  [ https://substack.com/redirect/de6146ee-f240-48b8-b753-88321468825a?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ](HF0), Tutti AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/69dfb9cf-eb4c-4017-9e8b-1efc26ee737f?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] (Berkley SkyDeck), Openmart [ https://substack.com/redirect/78d89dad-ab0c-485e-bbaa-d1f17b5ace27?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] (Y Combinator), VideoTutor ($11M Seed), Insforge [ https://substack.com/redirect/86b31af4-9f94-468b-8291-1358a5db1170?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] ($1.5 M Pre-seed), SCAM AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/4e6971bb-ada2-4267-a0d8-fe43575fa5f0?j=eyJ1IjoiM3J0OW5nIn0.sEw6r2ua7eqZyk0Tqslbdm-LriK_wFQad1xrjGr2Pek ] (Berkeley SkyDeck), and more.
â¨ Cohort 13 is now open. The founders of tomorrow are building today. Apply to Cohort 13 to secure your spot now.
Cheers,
Beta University Team

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZXRhdW5pdmVyc2l0eS5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_.XX7ETK-NKNwNO2a8RgKkV9KnFbkQ7oN3bIz9q6uAzII?

emails.subject: Is your AI integration layer optimized for generative search?
emails.body:
Hi Moe,

Following up on my last email, I wanted to highlight another area where CSP's GEO audit could benefit Pica. Given your role as an integration companion, establishing strong third-party validation and thought leadership is crucial for AI search visibility.

For example, we helped an AI startup boost their AI search traffic by 215% by optimizing their EEAT signals. Our audit would provide Pica with actionable insights to strengthen your digital reputation.

I'd be happy to walk you through how we can tailor this to Pica's unique needs. If this isn't the right time, just say the word, and I'll respect your decision.

Best regards,
Chris
CEO & Founder, CSP
Â 


On Mon, Feb 2, 2026 at 7:08 PM Chris Rodgers <message@sendwire.info> wrote:



> Hi Moe,
> 
> I came across Pica's work in streamlining AI agent and SaaS integrations. It's impressive how you're simplifying connectivity for users. Given your focus on seamless workflows, I wanted to share how CSP's Generative Engine Optimization audit could further enhance your visibility in AI-driven search.
> 
> GEO is the next frontier in digital presence. It ensures your brand is discoverable not just on traditional search engines but also in generative platforms like ChatGPT and Google's AI Overviews. Our audit evaluates critical areas like digital identity consistency, which is key for AI recognition.
> 
> With over 100 years of combined SEO expertise, we've helped brands like yours stay ahead in the AI search landscape. I'd love to discuss how we can identify gaps and opportunities specific to Pica.
> 
> I really think there's a great opportunity here to elevate Pica's visibility in generative search. If you don't see the value, just let me know, and I won't follow up further.
> 
> Best regards,
> Chris
> CEO & Founder, CSP

emails.subject: Re: Need help
emails.body:
Hi Sei and Lou,

Thank you for reaching out.

After you have signed up for PicaOS with the AppSumo plan, please email us
so that we can manually set a cancellation date for the billing period.
This will ensure the account is correctly provisioned under the AppSumo
entitlement and that no future charges will occur.

Best regards,

Support Team


On Tue, Feb 10, 2026 at 3:40â¯PM AppSumo Support <support@appsumo.com> wrote:

> *Hello PicaOS Team,*
>
> Weâre reaching out on behalf of our mutual customer, Lou, who redeemed
> PicaOS via the AppSumo Build Faster Bundle and is seeking clarification
> regarding their account billing status.
>
> After redemption, the customerâs PicaOS account shows 100% off for the
> first month only, with a future charge scheduled. Since this access was
> redeemed through an AppSumo bundle, the customer would like confirmation
> that their account is correctly provisioned under the AppSumo entitlement
> and that no future billing will occur beyond whatâs included in the bundle.
>
> Could you please help confirm:
>
>
>    - Whether the customerâs account is properly attached to the AppSumo
>    bundle entitlement, and
>    - That they will not be charged after the initial period shown in
>    their dashboard.
>
>
> Weâve CCâd the customer here so they can follow along directly.
>
> Thank you for your help, and we appreciate your support in getting this
> clarified promptly.
>
> Best regards,
>
>
>
> Sei Rosario
> support@appsumo.com
>
> *How likely are you to return to AppSumo for your future software needs?*
> Already back in the store
> <https://secure.helpscout.net/satisfaction/855916120/record/9830864979/1/>
>   Eh, I'm not sure
> <https://secure.helpscout.net/satisfaction/855916120/record/9830864979/2/>
>   Not likely
> <https://secure.helpscout.net/satisfaction/855916120/record/9830864979/3/>
> {#HS:3225229342-1240295#}
> On Tue, Feb 10, 2026 at 6:06 AM CST, Lou Val <fabcarden@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> One more thing the bundle I purchased for some reason is saying that
>>
>> "The refund window for this purchase ended on February 10, 2026. While we
>> can't process a refund at this time, we'd still like to offer our support."
>>
>> [image: ii_19c476eeb967857e2dd1]
>>
>> But the entire time when I originally signed up today Feb 10 2026 it said
>> I had 60 days for a refund... what is going on?
>>
>> How does that work?
>>
>> How did I lose 60 days in one hour?
>>
>> On Tue, Feb 10, 2026 at 6:00 AM CST, Lou Val <fabcarden@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>>
>>> Hi AppSumo Support Team,
>>>
>>> I purchased the Build Faster bundle and am running into multiple
>>> redemption issues across different tools in the bundle. I want to flag
>>> these early so they can be resolved correctly.
>>>
>>> Here's what I'm seeing so far:
>>>
>>> 1. Hostinger
>>>
>>> I followed the redemption instructions, but the promo code would not
>>> apply during checkout. I tried different plans and billing options, but it
>>> still wouldn't work. It shows the code doesn't exist. It's unclear whether
>>> this is a plan restriction, account issue, or a redemption integration
>>> problem.
>>>
>>> 2. PicaOS
>>>
>>> After redeeming, my PicaOS account shows 100% off for the first month
>>> only, with a charge scheduled for the following month. Since this was
>>> redeemed through AppSumo, I want to confirm that my account is properly
>>> attached to the AppSumo entitlement (lifetime/partner access) and that I
>>> will not be billed after the first month.

>>>

>>>
>>> Luis Valadez
>>>

emails.subject: RE: message for Moe
emails.body:
Hi Moe,

Here's what I keep thinking about: your customers aren't just looking for better tools. They're looking for permission to believe that fixing their workflows is actually possible.

A book does something Slack posts and demo videos can't. It says "here's the thinking behind this." It builds trust before they ever sign up.

Right now, MuleSoft and Workato own the narrative in this space. A book written by you would be the thing people reference when they're deciding whether integration work is worth the effort.

We'd handle the heavy lifting. You'd just need to share what you already know.

Want to explore what that might look like?


Bobby Goodwin
Founder
Branding by books

On Fri, Feb 6, 2026 at 02:04 AM UTC Bobby Goodwin <bobby@readermediahub.com> wrote:

> Hi Moe,
> 
> Building Pica must feel like solving a puzzle for people constantly buried in tasks. Helping them untangle their workflows? Thatâs a game-changer.
> 
> Ever think about writing a book about it? For the professionals you serve, a book could be more than just words. It could feel like a roadmap - timeless, trusted, and always on their shelf.
> 
> We make the whole process easy. Minimal time, maximum clarity.
> 
> Interested?
> 
> 
> Bobby Goodwin
> Founder
> Branding by books

emails.subject: [Array VC] Exciting Updates, Investments, Market Trends, & AI Opportunities
emails.body:
View this post on the web at https://insights.array.vc/p/array-vc-exciting-updates-investments

At Array Ventures [ https://substack.com/redirect/7ef4df8f-e00c-4a03-8a6f-18cc2d431e2c?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] have had quite an active last few months and there are no signs of slowing down! We are hiring to keep up with our growing agents! Please apply here [ https://substack.com/redirect/cc832503-f120-4d48-b9a4-a5c40b1fafa2?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] if you are a growth hacker interested in Venture Ops!
Portfolio News & Exits
Wabi.ai announced their $20M preseed [ https://substack.com/redirect/1174f5f1-ab8a-4b36-8046-81ced0e6b099?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] from a16z and have been going viral. Check out how 8 year olds are building [ https://substack.com/redirect/318ea4ea-4260-4a4f-b01e-5a4571a7499c?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] with Wabi and go build your app!
Sapiom.ai raises $15M from Accel [ https://substack.com/redirect/1b270fab-b17d-4ca4-bfc7-06ebfeafb9b3?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] to help agents buy their own tools
Catch Flamingo [ https://substack.com/redirect/10d2b590-c64a-4bb6-a4bc-956dc62490bf?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] on MSP Chat podcast [ https://substack.com/redirect/49fa0457-e45e-406b-80c4-ddac51823f3c?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] to hear why customers love Flamingoâs OpenSource AI Stack.
Congrats to Chisel [ https://substack.com/redirect/96110234-bfca-4a6a-bd84-c461b0341101?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] on their acquisition by Pendo [ https://substack.com/redirect/96110234-bfca-4a6a-bd84-c461b0341101?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] to help product teams build with agentic AI
Congrats to  [ https://substack.com/redirect/4c10744a-49fb-46d2-a443-8b21c9c8f6d9?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]Tellen [ https://substack.com/redirect/4c10744a-49fb-46d2-a443-8b21c9c8f6d9?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] on acquiring a large Grant Thorntonâs qm.x audit quality solution!
Hotdata is building a foundational piece of the agent data stack so agents can query data in milliseconds without moving data. Contribute to their project [ https://substack.com/redirect/34a5bfa3-02e2-464d-bfc7-0be45b17441c?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]!
Retina Robotics showed off their barcode-free inventory tracking [ https://substack.com/redirect/ff79d07e-61c1-4109-8567-2a99c2d9be2c?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] at Manifest!
Runableâs successful launch with over 1M views [ https://substack.com/redirect/4008fc60-3b58-4f72-89ac-cc0922f04462?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] lets people build anything like your next deck, website, or podcast!
Expanso [ https://substack.com/redirect/8e81f792-f4d6-47ec-833b-e0400f6c807a?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] shared a zero-trust data access layer for AI agents and share their OpenClaw integration
Leapfin announces $12M raise from Crosslink [ https://substack.com/redirect/50d0b649-50a5-4623-bf23-089e0904118c?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] to empower businesses with financial data.
CandorIQ rolled out a benchmarking product [ https://substack.com/redirect/ca38abe1-6fd6-4c61-9842-87191ee2f183?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] that helps companies understand how competitively theyâre paying their employees. Also check out their agent management HR [ https://substack.com/redirect/0b5957ad-596c-4195-82fd-3506b1de9000?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] tool!
RM Growth [ https://substack.com/redirect/ab3c1ef1-da1e-41cb-ad43-ac17621a028f?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] is making waves with it simplified social-commerce tools. One fleet of AI agents that take over everything creators and sellers do manually today from managing group chats to coordinating orders to let sellers sell on any platform at scale.
Decision Computing has been publishing the playbook for building mission-critical AI agents safe in their latest [ https://substack.com/redirect/4e9c3054-cedc-46de-ae5c-0e20c5edcdc1?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] posts.
Enterprises run table top exercises to stress test their cybersecurity measures and Reflex Security [ https://substack.com/redirect/32a1fbf5-045e-4e4a-a843-137e6d0c8bea?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] enables enterprises to simulate such attacks.
HappyRobot [ https://substack.com/redirect/2edb19b5-52fa-42e6-aa16-8c39bebd04bd?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] shared their partnership with DHL, C. H. Robinson, Mode Global and talk about how to make supply chain safer  [ https://substack.com/redirect/4ab69112-8996-4f8f-a0de-201c8718be08?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]at Manifest!
Blaze [ https://substack.com/redirect/0edc744f-5b73-49a1-a8e8-9ade99f6fc37?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] recently opened access to autonomous multi-agent AI marketer that strategizes, creates, posts, and learns ongoing marketing content on behalf of users.
Runway [ https://substack.com/redirect/ed931f75-e217-4cec-a11d-228688d35bd4?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] launched FlightPaths, which gives teams unified visibility into all mobile app releases and lets them perform bulk actions and automations across apps.
Catch the  [ https://substack.com/redirect/a2a2fdb9-13b8-4fe7-8732-421018032e59?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]Sotto AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/a2a2fdb9-13b8-4fe7-8732-421018032e59?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] founder [ https://substack.com/redirect/a2a2fdb9-13b8-4fe7-8732-421018032e59?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] talking about how the ticketing industry can  market using AI.
Array AI Labs
Weâve been actively experimenting with the latest AI products, integrating them into our stack, and sharing our learnings online. Being early adopters is helping us stay close to where the market is moving. 
Weâve also been able to spot and highlight some deeply buried features in new AI tools before they became widely known. 
The Array Dashboard is now the Array Operating System:The Array dashboard got a major upgrade. We moved from a traditional dashboard setup to a fully agentic system that can take on multiple tasks beyond just operations. We now have specialized agents for marketing, reporting, and several other functions. These agents can function alone or in swarms and coordinate + communicate to get tasks done.  
Weâre not just investing in the AI stack. Weâre running it. Six portfolio companies are already embedded in how we operate day to day, from Hotdataâs federated data layer to Meibelâs runtime orchestration, Sapiom for payments, Wokelo for research, Agency for our meetings intelligence, and Runable for AI-powered content and repeated tasks. Our investment thesis comes from what survives our own workflows.
Jobs
Find jobs at Array VC [ https://substack.com/redirect/e6f94a34-f723-4c79-945a-406dab607053?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] companies directly on our website. Our companies are hiring across Engineering, Marketing, Sales, and many more roles. 
What Is Trending in Tech AI?
Multi-agent systems crossed the line from research to production this year. Specialized agents that own a domain, coordinate with each other, and run around the clock. Your next department hire will not be a person.
Shared Persistent Memory Infrastructure. Always-on agents generate thousands of decisions a day.
The UI layer is dissolving. Protocols like MCP-UI, AI-native app builders like LastApp [ https://substack.com/redirect/80d34891-201f-4ea1-afc0-07463c231023?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ], and agents that generate their own interfaces are killing the static dashboard. Your next internal tool might build itself.
Open models are now months behind the frontier, not years. Enterprises that need to own their stack are running both. 
Ads are entering AI. As agents handle purchases and decisions, who gets placed inside an agentâs decision flow is the next billion-dollar question.
AI is leaving the screen. World models and robotic simulation are making it possible to train machines in synthetic environments before they touch the real world.
The median burn multiple has fallen below 1.5Ã for the first time since 2020. Companies are being efficient, revenues are kicking in early. Enterprises and SMBs are willing to pay, pilots are converting.
What Are We Looking To Invest Right Now?
We published a list of things here [ https://substack.com/redirect/f80696cc-d254-4d79-8865-b495db3e99d9?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ] we are looking to invest and here are few additional things we will add. Email us deals@array.vc [ mailto:deals@array.vc ] if you are building in any of these areas.
Long-Running Agents That Handle Chaos. If agents are the new hires, they need to handle the same mess real employees do. Multi-day tasks, broken integrations, changing requirements. Weâre investing in the agents and infrastructure built for that reality.
Runtime Policy Engines. And just like employees, they need rules. Real-time guardrails that control what agents can do and self-correct when they go off track.
Shared persistent Memory Infrastructure for Agent swarms handling multiple tasks together as a team.
AI That Learns From How Humans Actually Work. Zero shot AI trained on Egocentric first-person footage of experts performing various tasks.
Multirobot Orchestration. An abstraction to operate, coordinate and perform tasks across a variety of robotic fleets, legacy robots and the latest AMRs.
Worldmodels. Physics-aware simulation engines that let robots, autonomous vehicles, and industrial systems train on thousands of synthetic scenarios before touching the real world. The infrastructure layer for building and deploying domain-specific world models.
Edge AI. The compression, runtime, and orchestration stack that makes every device intelligent without ever calling the cloud.
Consumer Interfaces. The next interface isnât chat or voice. AI-native hardware and software that rewires how humans interact with information, make decisions, and navigate the physical world. 
Array Ventures invests $250k-$3m checks at formation. If you are starting a company in AI Infrastructure, making any industry such as healthcare, finance, manufacturing, supply chain, etc. better. Email us at deals@array.vc.
Thanks
Array Ventures Team
Our content is getting interesting everyday with our AI experiments. You can get those updates in between these quarterly updates on X  [ https://substack.com/redirect/19597b99-4b5f-4d3d-891f-d0365e7aaceb?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ]and LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/9b527e51-991a-4dd9-9f66-bd09a350d914?j=eyJ1IjoiNWc4N2VvIn0.i1yBnIQu-yyG5WO7bOLXUfqLasCV2M4mch-39k2GODs ].

Unsubscribe https://substack..K-hr6C_7vnRFgZv03oHupXJNYQ7_noBq0hfiZN1B4P8?

Mastra Newsletter #41

On Monday we announced Observational Memory (https://d5DGBm04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/ZY+113/d5DGBm04/VWnvxt79JsWGW3xMz1X71kVGtVGwS3m5KpBR0N7swVw83lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3q1W2XFHX91fQJrnW6c_KcP8nCLSBW4bYXr_85PDrPW28Ng1Q45XJqMW415nvn6lmB60W2_h6q96hVqXfW6Nk_tW1lw9V2W2KCZ_h5LHgh3W2w7MTN8KyHPqW47vhsD2Wb4bfV_KyNQ8FB1mVN2PDSfZswrYTW1bkg3d8l01x5W3jmM1n8Q6bJTW40zCdp3lRL1jW8ZYMp81wkZ7wN64kFzpccFz0W23jlYq12-KBkVWkdnb7tlzF9W4KPY9B1sP-0yW4-b-Hw6xcdzmW6m5R4s7mn4Fbf3bTbxC04 ) (OM), a new memory system for AI agents that achieves 94.87% on LongMemEval with gpt-5-mini, more than 3 points higher than any previously recorded score.

In less than 48 hours, OM was covered by VentureBeat, and our announcement thread on X reached over 100,000 views and received more than 1,000 bookmarks.

Observational Memory is a human-like, text-based memory system built for agents with a completely stable context window. There is no vector or graph database to manage. It works with Anthropic and OpenAI prompt caching out of the box and is fully open-source.

Highlights

@mastra/core@1.3.0

Async buffering for Observational Memory is now enabled by default. Observations are pre-computed in the background as conversations grow,

keeping memory ready without adding latency.

Workspaces introduce CompositeFilesystem for mounting multiple storage providers under a single unified directory tree, with new @mastra/e2b for sandboxed code execution, and @mastra/s3 and @mastra/gcs packages for cloud storage.

The model router now includes official @ai-sdk/groq support, replacing the previous OpenAI-compatible fallback.

See the full release notes (https://d5DGBm04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/ZY+113/d5DGBm04/VWnvxt79JsWGW3xMz1X71kVGtVGwS3m5KpBR0N7swVw83lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3lXW13vBbJ7JjZDxW6prpvn2KclbVVLWK4F94xtJjW3f5ZbK7qzJB2W3ZbSZS5B7qBjW8ZKfF77zcq2tW6Pf2bc1Tgdz6Vbn40L4fbQt2W4FWyyh22cwB3W3j1Csq14KNzhW1092tz9286d-W84fjbP5jnBvtW3Mh4F-5Wd-PSW5D65kR2Jg-cbW3lBpDF5MdD7yW3rvPQ_46jG9gW5GqVQV51-pWLVH38D030h-tyW1NHSHX8dqc24W413dLr2_W0hpVVMSdq302KNWN2CMY0HHF0pCf7kvZg004 ) for all package changes.

Events

More Thursday workshops are listed on our events page (https://d5DGBm04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/ZY+113/d5DGBm04/VWnvxt79JsWGW3xMz1X71kVGtVGwS3m5KpBR0N7swVvT3lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3kHW7vpPL41xthGzW5Tns7159zrqsW762SbB1_ZFSTW1BNpPz2JlYG8W7qpSm08FdcgLW47yvxT3QdjTMW13f6Kq8ndJ2CW27sHHN2PrGskVyR8Zq4PJGnkVByDpP6c6J2sN8Rm6YVd3w-HW5TWWc96S0xjjW908W8P99-6zYW1--p5N4gP09CW43jwVj2g3-phW1G1Lh87K6rJ7W1CGYmN1GYcL1VvN3Gc2f9X-FW93mxFv3RR-hPW6pm7LW5HLVrZf4GnVgC04 ) .

Build agents with human-like memory

ð Thursday, Feb 12th PT: 9:00 â¢ ET: 12:00 â¢ GMT 17:00 â¢ CET: 18:00

common-workshop-alex-and-tyler-lg (https://d5DGBm04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/ZY+113/d5DGBm04/VWnvxt79JsWGW3xMz1X71kVGtVGwS3m5KpBR0N7swVvT3lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3nzVrpX2T96tPqWW16YBMw4yDlX-W5vRspc7mVQ_sW8Xm8sp6P-qSNW4wyJcs7GhrrfN1QqZy36Xv-6W3dv7222JcSjTMfy5J44FHCcW20mZ7d30YL6YW4sRWw37qq6tMW8dcqNy64znzGW9lb9ss7dmyvvW5X9_cH4fTqSmW3Np2s78BRBByN5NKdkwRq9GMW3TR5C76Q_TF7W1TTw4y7WFYZ2W385HTF19czsQW1L-Yh47JB12nVBNwnw54Frv8f6DyBTM04 )

Mastra Newsletter #41

On Monday we announced Observational Memory (https://d5DGBm04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/ZY+113/d5DGBm04/VX61Vq1wP22dW5wjQGQ15j6FGW8fXtNV5KpBJ2N6fpSWl3lcq-W7lCdLW6lZ3kqW28V7yP7xFklLVBYJFM3SpYgBN8vbDRM9DD0XW1Wc_Cn4W6HRdW2M2tH72mPTYwW3SBjy71bH43pW6p0mK_8wLD92W1VD6ym4LjSfWW3D51TT4Kw2BHN1GP2W2vqHP9V9Wpzv3bSrPFMW0_S8nXCcSW7pH0d35dL0jNW1NGdCd2nMtkMW6x7pb45NrkskW48TL2w5TMZrqW381lQd2vCSs8W5yl8Jv1GXTLhW729CH08Z2YGZW561D2C5p4jVCN52rRVYkqBfHW4sw8Vr8l6CWGW8p_dkF7Kw3dJW5TtlRR4Tw-RNf7zJvTR04 ) (OM), a new memory system for AI agents that achieves 94.87% on LongMemEval with gpt-5-mini, more than 3 points higher than any previously recorded score.

In less than 48 hours, OM was covered by VentureBeat, and our announcement thread on X reached over 100,000 views and received more than 1,000 bookmarks.

Observational Memory is a human-like, text-based memory system built for agents with a completely stable context window. There is no vector or graph database to manage. It works with Anthropic and OpenAI prompt caching out of the box and is fully open-source.

Highlights

@mastra/core@1.3.0

Async buffering for Observational Memory is now enabled by default. Observations are pre-computed in the background as conversations grow,

keeping memory ready without adding latency.

Workspaces introduce CompositeFilesystem for mounting multiple storage providers under a single unified directory tree, with new @mastra/e2b for sandboxed code execution, and @mastra/s3 and @mastra/gcs packages for cloud storage.

The model router now includes official @ai-sdk/groq support, replacing the previous OpenAI-compatible fallback.

See the full release notes (https://d5DGBm04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/ZY+113/d5DGBm04/VX61Vq1wP22dW5wjQGQ15j6FGW8fXtNV5KpBJ2N6fpSW23lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3pXW1GCZnv3Slyh_N4k2gMdWnKNQW5yr0sm6Vf-SjW45mT0S5bKnrcW9lJPld86WcKzW9btLzd3yBcjTW7nYBv86LyhSxW6S86M63HK72WW6ngqhr6_C67wV_-8Kp7QDgVQW2VNrTc8kfPP9W3-b9L77pWVkbW49DQgQ6_7PFHW4jTgZ499jJp8N400fqr4pB3RW60_3Hf5HRDXqW4Bsqwx5fZZbqT8w0K2blZmsW4cx18-2Rl2sXW2lc12f7g6DCWW7VVqFS99jZl6W1bZnGh4215H9f175fq004 ) for all package changes.

Events

More Thursday workshops are listed on our events page (https://d5DGBm04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/ZY+113/d5DGBm04/VX61Vq1wP22dW5wjQGQ15j6FGW8fXtNV5KpBJ2N6fpSVM3lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3nBW4QTjNz3GlncYW7qKSGl3fT-2RW72XKkj3fs2qDMXbWFJGjztlW8X0xvg8nN5K7W8FvHq72fQl52W95-nKQ8zGK2bW3tP95F2ShWVNW3FhXgR1Kjq9cW6PphGp3c_B5-W3zjw7H5S72QqW6KMbk76ZjgKxW5wRqHQ6bxXsyW3Qr0rF98HJDrW32M7D26NhPxJW2hZcg-8CkTYGW4dF5Rf6mfV-DW5qTJLD6WP2cyW4lXc6V2FKFZ8W3-G8QX4pxFfkf3q9g5F04 ) .

emails.subject: Still thinking about MXP? Here's what you'd experience on May 12.
emails.body:
divider

Â 

Mixpanel ( https://mixpanel.com/?utm_campaign=MXP+San+Francisco+Email+Blast&utm_content=Email+2+%28non+registrants%29&utm_medium=email&utm_source=customerio )

Events

( Mixpanel.com/mxp/mxp-2026?utm_source=event&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=EV-2026-05-12-MXPSF-NA-FM&utm_content=email blast&utm_campaign=MXP+San+Francisco+Email+Blast&utm_content=Email+2+%28non+registrants%29&utm_medium=email&utm_source=customerio )Not sure if MXP is worth your time? Let us show you what your day in San Francisco could look like.

We noticed you havenât registered yet, that's okay. Maybe you're still considering if MXP is right for you.

Â 

Here's the thing: MXP isn't a typical conference. You don't sit through endless pitches or boring, unrelated panels. You choose your own path based on the problems you actually want to solve.

Where: Terra Gallery, San Francisco
When: May 12, 2026

Â 

Here's how your day would unfold:

Opening keynote from Jen Taylor, Mixpanel CEO & Anant Gupta, Mixpanel CTO

Jen and Anant will unveil Mixpanelâs vision and product roadmap â brought to life through real customer stories. Be the first to know whatâs coming and how itâll impact teams like yours.

Leadership Tracks: Make smarter calls, faster

Executive-grade sessions led by respected product and data leaders. Expect honest perspectives on real trade offs â the kind of conversations that change how you think about your business.

Mixpanel Masters: Hands-on problem solving with product experts

Live deep dives where Mixpanel experts demonstrate specific product features by solving real problems on the spot. Bring your actual use cases, work hands-on with the platform, and leave knowing exactly what to build when you get back to the office.

Community and product experiences: Build your go-to network

Small-group roundtables with practitioners who've faced your exact challenges. Test AI-powered features in our product zones. Leave with a shortlist of people who get what you're dealing with and will pick up when you call.

Plus: A pre-event hackathon, executive dinners, and networking receptions throughout the week.

The difference? You pick what matters to you.

Come for the workshops. Network as much, or as little, as you want. Dive into the sessions that interest you. It's your day, your way.

Â 

Spots are filling up. If you're on the fence, now's the time to decide.

Register Now ( Mixpanel.com/mxp/mxp-2026?utm_source=event&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=EV-2026-05-12-MXPSF-NA-FM&utm_content=email blast&utm_campaign=MXP+San+Francisco+Email+Blast&utm_content=Email+2+%28non+registrants%29&utm_medium=email&utm_source=customerio )

mixpanel

emails.subject: Re: FRV Co-working days
emails.body:
Tuesdays are good for me â I'll be there

Sent via Superhuman ( https://sprh.mn/?vip=derek@superframe.com )

On Tue, Feb 10, 2026 at 5:44â¯PM, Dan Scholnick < dan@fourriversgroup.com > wrote:

> 
> Hi SF-based FRV Founders,
> 
> 
> I am planning to host FRV co-working days at my home coffee room. I invite
> you to come over to work, meet your fellow SF founders, and bounce ideas
> off each other. I will provide coffee and pastries, as well as lunch for
> those who stay through the afternoon.
> 
> 
> This is entirely optional, and there is no pressure to attend. My plan is
> to schedule a standing co-working day once every other month on a Tuesday.
> If there is strong interest, we can increase the frequency; if not, we can
> adjust accordingly. If Tuesday is a difficult day for most of you, please
> let me know.
> 
> 
> For our first session, I would like everyone to give a brief product demo
> to familiarize the group with your respective companies.
> 
> 
> Best,
> 
> 
> Dan
>

You can find our community team on the 8th Floor.
Getting here

Entrance address
Please use this address to access this location:
2 Embarcadero Center, 8th Floor
San Francisco, California 94111

Entrance instructions
From the street level, take the escalators up to the security lobby. After getting through the turnstiles, take the elevator bank on your right-hand side and come up to the 8th floor. Our community bar is located to the right of the open doors, out in the common area! If you have a bike and need to store it, our bike room on the 8th floor is open starting at 8AM. Please find elevator car 7, located on street level toward the middle of the building. Call the intercom and ask the security team to be let up to the 8th floor anytime between 8AM to 5PM, Monday through Friday.

Parking
Parking Garages are available in all Embarcadero Centers (1, 2, 3, & 4). We unfortunately can not validate parking. Please feel free to utilize other parking options in the area if you don't mind a short walk! Street parking is also available but extremely limited.

emails.subject: Shared Workspace booking is confirmed for Wednesday, February 11 at Two Embarcadero Center
emails.body:
Your desk is booked, Moe Katib!


If you plan to bring guests to this space, a private office or conference room booking is required. Please note that the number of guests is limited to the capacity of the room booked.
When:
Wednesday, February 11, 2026
6:00â¯AM - 11:59â¯PM
Where:
2 Embarcadero Center, 8th Floor San Francisco, CA 94111

Cost:Included
For your booking

Guest policy
At this time, desk bookings are valid for one person per account. All guests will be required to book their own desk.
When you arrive
You can find our community team on the 8th Floor.
Getting here

Entrance address
Please use this address to access this location:
2 Embarcadero Center, 8th Floor
San Francisco, California 94111

Entrance instructions
From the street level, take the escalators up to the security lobby. After getting through the turnstiles, take the elevator bank on your right-hand side and come up to the 8th floor. Our community bar is located to the right of the open doors, out in the common area! If you have a bike and need to store it, our bike room on the 8th floor is open starting at 8AM. Please find elevator car 7, located on street level toward the middle of the building. Call the intercom and ask the security team to be let up to the 8th floor anytime between 8AM to 5PM, Monday through Friday.

Parking
Parking Garages are available in all Embarcadero Centers (1, 2, 3, & 4). We unfortunately can not validate parking. Please feel free to utilize other parking options in the area if you don't mind a short walk! Street parking is also available but extremely limited.
Policies
Desk policy
The WeWork All Access membership allows members to book one desk booking per day, subject to availability, at hundreds of WeWork locations globally. Additional same-day desk booking incurs a cost of 1 credit.

Desk bookings provide access to pantries, lounges, phone booths, and shared common areas; a separate booking is required to access meeting rooms.

Cancellation policy
Desk bookings may be canceled by 11:59pm the day before the booking starts. Bookings made day-of can be canceled within 5 minutes of making a booking.

For further details read our Member Policy Guidelines.

Prioritizing health & safety
Get familiar with the detailed arrangements we have in place to deal with potential emergency situations in our Emergency Action Plan and report any incidents or dangerous occurrences to the Community Team without delay.
Member Help
Privacy PolicyHouse Rules
Data Connection & Internet Access Terms of Services

Hereâs what you missed on Contra

1. Paula | Land it
Love the Contra version of Wrapped ð¥ I can't believe we're part of the Elite Earners club ð¥¹

Since I got a couple of questions on tips to grow as a freelancer/agency owner, dropping some insights from this year ð 

- It's okay to let clients go because they don't treat you right or give you anxiety
- We had the best financial year, but it didn't come without me chasing clients to pay us, sending proposals that got ghosted, applying to opportunities, strategizing new ways to grow, etc
- As a lot of people say, networking is key. I attended around 4 conferences this year and connected with many people.
- LinkedIn is where it's at! I've had clients reach out to me because I decided to add my current and past clients as experience on LinkedIn ð

Thank you to everyone and hope you have an amazing holiday season ð«¶ð» 
[Image attached]

emails.subject: February admin update
emails.body:
Slack
 
 





 


 

 
 



  


https://click.email.slackhq.com/? 


February â¢ 2026
 

Slack Admin Updates
 

Read on to prepare yourselves and your members for new capabilities, changes and deprecations.
 

WHAT'S NEW


  

Slackbot (Business+, Enterprise+)

Weâve redesigned Slackbot to be your personal AI agent integrated directly into Slack. It searches your permissioned conversations and files, analyzes documents, manages calendars, and creates structured content such as canvases and meeting notes. It requires no setup or additional tools, operates through direct messages, and uses workspace context to support tasks ranging from finding information to generating documentation.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

AI suggested channel sections (Business+, Enterprise+)

A new feature recommends existing sidebar sections when members join new channels. Instead of all new channels landing in the default Channels section, suggestions are based on the channelâs content and the memberâs existing organization.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Message triggers in private channels

Members can create no-code workflows that trigger when specific keywords are posted in private channels. Previously, this capability was limited to public channels. Admins must enable this feature, as private channels are off by default.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Create org-wide admin groups (Enterprise plans)

Org Owners and Admins can now create and manage org-level groups directly from the org dashboard. These groups can be created manually or via CSV upload and can be utilized to assign the groups to roles, feature access controls, workspaces, and channels. All IDP and Slack-created groups now appear together in the Groups section of the org dashboard. This gives admins flexibility to manage access without relying solely on IDP syncing.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Split view on desktop

Split view allows members to pin a thread, channel, canvas, or other content in a static, side-by-side view while continuing to navigate in the main area of the desktop app.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

AI exclusions (Enterprise+)

AI exclusions allow Admins to mark specific channels, canvases, and lists as excluded from AI, preventing their content from being used in features like Slackbot AI and AI-powered search.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Alt text reminders

Members and Admins can enable alt text reminders that prompt for image descriptions during upload. All image thumbnails display a badge showing whether alt text has been added.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Slack Actions for Flows V2

Six additional Salesforce Flow actions are now available, completing parity with legacy Connected App-based actions and extending Flow capabilities to match Agent actions. This builds on the initial five Flow actions launched in v1.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Security warnings for forwarded emails

When an email is forwarded into Slack via a channel email address or the Slackbot forwarded email feature and fails important security checks (DMARC, SPF, or DKIM validation), it will now display a clear security warning on the message.


  

Canvas AI analytics

Weâve added a new canvas AI section to the AI analytics dashboard where admins can track the daily/weekly/monthly count of users who submit AI prompts in canvases (including followups/refinements), daily/weekly/month count of number of AI submissions, and the average feature usage over 28-day periods.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Add custom sources to enterprise search

The Enterprise Search for apps feature enables real-time search for users across multiple external sources. Developers can use this feature to integrate their organization's internal data sources into Slack (for example, wikis and proprietary systems). By connecting searchable in-house data, users can access this information directly within Slack, creating a comprehensive search experience


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →


COMING SOON*
 

  

Updates to Activity

The new Activity hub will consolidate all notification types into a single feed and replace the current sidebar layout with a flexible page design that supports side-by-side views and customizable density options. The update will add triage tools including filtering, sorting, and bulk actions.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Slack MCP server

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a protocol that lets AI clients securely access data sources. MCP servers implement that protocol for specific tools. The Slack MCP server provides secure access to your Slack workspace, allowing AI agents and other integrations to interact with your team's data.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

  

Slack RTS API

Slackâs public real-time search (RTS) API will enable external third-party apps to search Slack data in real-time without storing or indexing the data locally.


  

Pre-Migration bulk email updates

Admins will be able to configure bulk email updates prior to an enterprise migration.


  

Edit attachments after sending a message

Users will have the ability to edit attachments after sending a message.


  

Upcoming deprecations

  

Audit log retention policy is changing starting April 30th, 2026

From April 30, 2026, we will transition to a two-year retention policy for customer Audit Logs. From this date,we will start to remove any logs older than two years. You can export your data and save it elsewhere by using our Audit Log API or UI. This change is only for the audit log feature and API, and does not impact a teamâs retention


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →

OS, app, and browser versions reaching end of support on May 18, 2026

In accordance with our support lifecycle policy, we have discontinued support for a number of operating systems, app versions, and browsers.


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Learn more ‌ →


 


https://click.email.slackhq.com/?%3D%3D 
Help Center 

*Purchase of Slack services is not dependent on the delivery of any future functionality, and such functionality is subject to change.
 


 

 


 
 
https://click.email.slackhq.com/?

San Francisco, CA 94105, United States
